commands.review_summary:
The PR adds purpose-specific memory role slots, legacy memory-slot migration, per-agent memory slot overrides, and corresponding docs/tests across config, plugin activation, runtime, doctor/status, and plugin surfaces.

PR surface: Source +896, Tests +901, Docs +22, Generated 0. Total +1819 across 71 files.

A config with a global compaction slot plus an agent override would still use the global provider in that runtime path.

target.title: Doctor: structure shell completion repair
target.body:
## Summary

This is the intentionally small doctor repair migration example.

- keeps the public doctor/plugin SDK contracts unchanged
- keeps normal `openclaw doctor` shell-completion behavior on the existing `doctorShellCompletion()` path
- adds a core health check for shell completion so lint can report slow dynamic completion and missing completion caches
- lets `openclaw doctor --fix` run shell completion through the structured repair runner
- keeps shell completion out of the broad structured repair pass so the positional doctor contribution still controls ordering and presentation

The shape is meant to be the repeatable pattern for low-risk doctor migrations: same rule behavior, small internal wrapper, tests doing most of the work.

Diff shape after rebuilding on current `main`:

- 9 files changed
- 623 insertions, 99 deletions
- production code: 282 insertions, 98 deletions
- tests: 341 insertions, 1 deletion

## Verification

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/health-check-registry.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/health-check-registry.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot` - 5 files, 53 tests
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
- `bash /mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 4 constrained provider capabilities
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR keeps WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles honest by making hosted, device-bound, self-hosted, Mac-hosted, and bridge-backed constraints visible instead of pretending they are equivalent official bot APIs. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 4 - Constrained/device-bound provider capabilities - Role: Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. - Head: `c4ecab1e5c` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `c4ecab1e5c`. Focused local validation after the latest stack hardening ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` - `pnpm format:docs:check` - `pnpm lint:docs` - `pnpm docs:check-mdx` - `pnpm docs:check-links` - `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` skipped locally because this worktree had no merge-base context; GitHub Actions remains the broad docs authority. - `git diff --check` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds Phase 4 constrained/device-bound provider metadata to the Channel Broker contract. - Covers WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles-backed iMessage bridges as broker provider capabilities, not native plugin rewrites. - Stacks on Phase 3 official/app capability matrix (#86164), Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86114 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86148, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds `constraints` and `badges` to broker platform/provider capabilities. - Models constrained provider facts such as: - `businessApi`, `cloudApi`, `providerHosted` - `deviceBound`, `linkedDevice`, `qrPairing`, `sessionFragile` - `selfHosted`, `phoneNumberRequired`, `signalCli` - `macHostRequired`, `messagesSignedIn`, `privateApiOptional`, `privateApiRequired` - `externalBridge` - Exposes resolved provider capabilities in channel-broker status snapshots so UI/status can show constrained-provider badges. - Adds WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage target proof through `channel-broker`. - Documents constrained provider expectations in Channel Broker, SDK, WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles migration docs. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Constrained providers expose hosted, device-bound, self-hosted, Mac-hosted, and bridge-backed limits through broker `constraints`/`badges`, and WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage still route through provider-owned broker requests. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that evaluated constrained-provider capability support and called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` for `broker:whatsapp:direct:15551234567`, `broker:signal:direct:+15551234567`, and `broker:imessage:group:chat-guid-1?threadId=imessage-thread-1`. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "constrainedProviders": { "whatsappBusiness": true, "whatsappDeviceBound": false, "signalDeviceBound": true }, "outbound": [ { "label": "whatsapp-direct", "platform": "whatsapp", "conversation": { "id": "15551234567", "type": "direct" } }, { "label": "signal-direct", "platform": "signal", "conversation": { "id": "+15551234567", "type": "direct" } }, { "label": "imessage-group", "platform": "imessage", "conversation": { "id": "chat-guid-1", "type": "group", "threadId": "imessage-thread-1" } } ] } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live SDK/plugin path distinguished WhatsApp Business-style support from device-bound WhatsApp requirements, accepted Signal's self-hosted/device-bound constraint set, and produced broker provider requests for WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage. - **What was not tested:** Live WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, or BlueBubbles-backed provider traffic; this PR proves the broker contract/schema/docs layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/status.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/types.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not implement Phase 5 legacy-plugin freeze. - Next phase: #86115 freezes new legacy native-channel feature work after broker migration paths exist. This PR supplies the constrained-provider metadata needed before that freeze can be credible.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 3 official channel capability matrix
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR prevents the long tail from becoming a new pile of native branches. It expresses official app/API platforms as broker aliases and capability rows first, and it now enforces declared delivery capabilities before outbound handoff. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 3 - Official/app platform capability matrix - Role: Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. - Head: `15aab904e7` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `15aab904e7`. Focused local validation after the latest stack hardening ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` - `pnpm format:docs:check` - `pnpm lint:docs` - `pnpm docs:check-mdx` - `pnpm docs:check-links` - `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` skipped locally because this worktree had no merge-base context; GitHub Actions remains the broad docs authority. - `git diff --check` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 3 official/app channel capability matrix for Channel Broker. - Keeps the work at the broker contract/catalog layer: no native channel migration defaults change in this PR. - Stacks on Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86113 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86144, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds canonical broker platform aliases while keeping `platform: string` open for provider-owned long-tail targets: - `teams` / `msteams` -> `microsoft-teams` - `googlechat` -> `google-chat` - `qq` -> `qqbot` - Aligns `channels.channel-broker.*.capabilities` config with the SDK capability shape: - `delivery` - `live` - `receive` - `native` - Adds focused proof for Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, LINE, Feishu, QQ bot, Zalo, Mattermost, Nextcloud Talk, Twitch, IRC, Nostr, Tlon, and Synology Chat. - Documents the Phase 3 platform matrix and SDK capability shape. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Official/app channel aliases normalize through the broker catalog, and Phase 3 platforms route through provider-owned broker requests without enabling native migration defaults. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:teams:19:meeting-channel"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "teams-alias", "messageId": "native-teams-alias", "platform": "microsoft-teams", "conversation": { "id": "19:meeting-channel", "type": "channel" }, "requirements": { "text": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path canonicalized the Teams alias to `microsoft-teams` and produced a provider-owned broker request with no platform-specific native extension branch. - **What was not tested:** Live Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, regional, or self-hosted provider traffic; this PR proves the broker catalog/schema/conformance layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` Note: `pnpm exec markdownlint-cli2 ...` was unavailable in the local workspace, so the documented `pnpm dlx` path was used. It linted the docs glob cleanly. ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not move native routing defaults for any Phase 3 channel.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 2D Slack migration proof
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR proves Slack DMs and channel threads can route through broker-owned semantics while native Slack remains the default path. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2D - Slack opt-in broker routing proof - Role: Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. - Head: `cf14b47e31` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `cf14b47e31`. Focused local validation for this phase is documented below. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. Native channel defaults remain unchanged unless this phase explicitly says otherwise. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2D Slack migration proof for the Channel Broker rollout. - Keeps native Slack default behavior unchanged; this only proves opt-in broker routing for Slack targets. - Stacks on Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86112 Related #86108 Related #86105 Supersedes closed PR #86142, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Normalize broker-prefixed Slack DM targets such as `broker:slack:user:U12345678` into broker provider requests with `conversation.type = "direct"`. - Normalize broker-prefixed Slack channel thread targets such as `broker:slack:channel:C12345678?threadId=1716500000.000001` into broker provider requests with `conversation.type = "channel"` and `threadId`. - Canonicalize Slack broker session routes to the resolved platform target, preserving direct-route semantics as `slack:direct%3A` and thread routes as `slack:?threadId=`. ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Slack DM and threaded-channel targets can route through `channel-broker` while preserving direct/channel conversation semantics and Slack `threadTs` as broker `threadId`. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:slack:channel:C12345678?threadId=1716500000.000001"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "slack-thread", "messageId": "native-slack-thread", "platform": "slack", "conversation": { "id": "C12345678", "type": "channel", "threadId": "1716500000.000001" }, "requirements": { "text": true, "thread": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path produced the expected Slack provider request and kept threaded delivery represented by the broker `threadId` field. - **What was not tested:** Live Slack API traffic and the full local suite; this PR stays scoped to opt-in broker routing proof. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - No native Slack migration is enabled by default in this PR. - Includes a tiny test-isolation hardening for `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 2C Discord migration proof
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR proves Discord DMs, channels, and threads can route through broker-owned semantics while native Discord remains the default path. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2C - Discord opt-in broker routing proof - Role: Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. - Head: `442d87e82b` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `442d87e82b`. Focused local validation for this phase is documented below. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. Native channel defaults remain unchanged unless this phase explicitly says otherwise. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2C Discord broker routing proof on top of #86154, #86153, and #86096. - Normalizes broker-routed Discord DMs and channel threads without importing native Discord extension internals. - Preserves non-channel conversation type in canonical broker routes so Discord DMs remain direct routes. ## Rollout Context Closes #86111 Related #86108 Related #86105 Stacked on #86154 Stacked on #86153 Stacked on #86096 Supersedes closed PR #86139, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. Native Discord defaults stay unchanged. Existing prefix ownership still keeps `discord:` owned by the native Discord plugin when installed; this PR proves the opt-in broker route through `broker:discord:...` and the broker alias path when native Discord is absent. For review, the focused stack diff is: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Discord DM and threaded-channel targets can route through `channel-broker` while preserving direct/channel conversation semantics and provider-owned ids. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:discord:user:123456789012345678"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "discord-dm", "messageId": "native-discord-dm", "platform": "discord", "conversation": { "id": "123456789012345678", "type": "direct" }, "requirements": { "text": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path produced a direct Discord broker provider request rather than an ambiguous channel-looking target. - **What was not tested:** Live Discord API traffic and the full local suite; this PR stays scoped to opt-in broker routing proof. ### Behavior OpenClaw can represent Discord DMs and channel threads through `channel-broker` using stable broker semantics: provider platform `discord`, direct/channel conversation type, conversation id, and optional thread id. ### Environment - Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker` - Branch: `feat/channel-broker-discord-routing` - Head: `442d87e82b` (current Discord proof head) ### Steps 1. Sent a synthetic message to `broker:discord:user:123456789012345678`. 2. Verified the provider request used `platform: "discord"`, `conversation.type: "direct"`, and `conversation.id: "123456789012345678"`. 3. Sent a synthetic message to `broker:discord:channel:222222222222222222?threadId=333333333333333333`. 4. Verified the provider request used channel conversation id plus `threadId`. 5. Resolved the outbound session route for the Discord DM target. 6. Verified the route stayed direct and canonicalized to `discord:direct%3A123456789012345678`. ### Evidence - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - 2 files passed, 13 tests passed. - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - passed. - `git diff --check` - passed. ### Observed Result Discord DMs and threads now flow through broker target parsing and provider request semantics, not through core or native Discord extension branches. ### Not Tested - Live Discord API traffic; this PR is the opt-in broker migration proof, not a live provider connector. - Full local suite. Per `/Volumes/LEXAR` policy, broad validation is left to GitHub Actions. ## Current Stack Gate Context This branch is rebased on the current Phase 1 foundation head. Focused local validation is documented above or below; broad validation remains on GitHub Actions.

V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2A - Conformance and signed inbound baseline - Role: Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff. - Head: `d218c81d5c` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `d218c81d5c`. Focused local validation for this phase is documented below. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. Native channel defaults remain unchanged unless this phase explicitly says otherwise. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2A Channel Broker conformance baseline on top of #86096. - Extends `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` with inbound normalization, dedupe-key, provider capability normalization, and platform capability checks. - Adds bundled broker conformance coverage for durable inbound receive, durable final send receipts, retryable receipt failures, preview/progress capability gates, and `/verbose`/tool-message `channelData`. ## Rollout Context Closes #86109 Related #86108 Related #86105 Stacked on #86096 Supersedes closed PR #86133, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. This PR intentionally does not migrate Telegram, Discord, or Slack routing. Native defaults stay unchanged until the Phase 2B/2C/2D proofs land behind opt-in broker routing. For review, the focused stack diff is: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** The broker conformance baseline normalizes inbound events, builds durable dedupe keys, evaluates preview/progress capabilities, and sends outbound requests through the bundled broker lifecycle without platform-specific core branches. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that imported `src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts`, created a Telegram inbound event, built the durable dedupe key, evaluated Discord preview/progress capabilities, and sent broker outbound requests through `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text`. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "inboundDedupeKey": "acme:bot-main:telegram:live-proof-telegram-1", "previewProgressSupported": true } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live SDK/plugin path produced the expected durable inbound key and capability decision outside the Vitest harness, proving the Phase 2A contract surface can carry the normalized lifecycle facts that later channel migrations consume. - **What was not tested:** Live provider network traffic and the full local suite; those remain covered by later provider proofs and GitHub Actions. ### Behavior The broker contract now has a conformance harness for the lifecycle paths that regress most often across maintained chat channels: inbound normalization/dedupe, durable final sends, provider receipts, retry/failure propagation, preview/progress capability checks, and `/verbose` tool-message metadata. ### Environment - Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker` - Branch: `feat/channel-broker-conformance` - Head: `d218c81d5c` (current Phase 2A head) ### Steps 1. Normalized a synthetic Telegram inbound event through the SDK and accepted it through the durable inbound receive journal. 2. Replayed the same dedupe key while pending and after completion to prove duplicate/redelivery handling. 3. Sent a synthetic Slack final message through the bundled broker runtime and verified provider receipt commit material. 4. Returned a retryable Discord provider receipt and verified the send fails with structured receipt context. 5. Built preview/finalize broker requests and asserted Discord preview/progress support through declared capabilities. 6. Passed `/verbose` tool-message policy through broker `channelData` without platform-specific branching. ### Evidence - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - 1 file passed, 7 tests passed. - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - 3 files passed, 10 tests passed. - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - passed. - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - passed. - `git diff --check` - passed. ### Observed Result The SDK helpers normalize provider payloads without dropping native metadata, the bundled plugin refuses non-sent provider receipts as durable-send failures, and conformance tests prove the current broker path can represent the Phase 2 lifecycle requirements without changing user-visible channel defaults. ### Not Tested - Full local suite. Per `/Volumes/LEXAR` policy, broad validation is left to GitHub Actions. - Live Telegram, Discord, or Slack provider traffic. Those are intentionally scoped to Phase 2B/2C/2D. ## Current Stack Gate Context This branch is rebased on the current Phase 1 foundation head. Focused local validation is documented above or below; broad validation remains on GitHub Actions.

target.body:
## Summary

- Add an explicit diagnostic LLM content-capture policy for model-call diagnostics.
- Attach Codex and Pi model-call prompt history, output messages, system prompt, and tool definitions when OTEL content capture is enabled.
- Export Phoenix/OpenTelemetry-readable LLM attributes, including `gen_ai.input.messages`, `gen_ai.output.messages`, `gen_ai.system_instructions`, `gen_ai.tool.definitions`, `input.value`, and `output.value`.
- Add focused tests and a changelog entry.

## Verification

- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/diagnostic-llm-content.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
  - 5 test files passed
  - 302 tests passed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenTelemetry LLM spans now expose full prompt history, system instructions, model output, and tool definitions when `diagnostics.otel.captureContent` is explicitly enabled.

Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw gateway from this branch with Phoenix running at `http://127.0.0.1:6006` and the gateway at `http://127.0.0.1:18789`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the gateway with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BONJOUR=1 OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT=http://127.0.0.1:6006/v1/traces OTEL_SEMCONV_STABILITY_OPT_IN=gen_ai_latest_experimental node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway --force`, then sent two turns through `node --import tsx src/entry.ts agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:otel-llm-trace-validation-20260524-2247 ...`.

Evidence after fix: Phoenix span `openclaw.model.call` / trace `d398c193c8145f82e0ae63f6620c1095` contained both validation markers, prior assistant history, the final assistant response, system instructions, and 17 tool definitions.

Observed result after fix: The second LLM span input was `user -> assistant -> user`, included `OPENCLAW_OTEL_BUNDLED_TURN_ONE`, `OPENCLAW_OTEL_BUNDLED_TURN_TWO`, and `bundled tracing one ok`, and output contained `bundled tracing two ok`.

What was not tested: No broad CI/Crabbox run and no live Pi provider run; the Pi emission path is covered by focused unit tests.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 1 protocol foundation
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR is the narrow foundation. It creates the versioned broker SDK subpath and bundled channel-broker plugin, but it does not move Telegram, Discord, Slack, or any user defaults. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 1 - SDK and bundled broker foundation - Role: Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract. - Head: `9e55f11ef9` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `9e55f11ef9`. Focused local validation for this phase is documented below. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. Native channel defaults remain unchanged unless this phase explicitly says otherwise. ## Summary Adds Phase 1 of the Channel Broker consolidation program: a versioned `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` protocol surface, a bundled `channel-broker` channel plugin, provider-owned outbound request/receipt helpers, docs, and focused tests. This PR intentionally stays at the protocol/foundation layer. Telegram, Discord, Slack, and later channel migrations are tracked separately and should not be folded into this PR. ## Linked Context Closes #86107 Related #86105 Related #86108 Related #86109 Related #86110 Related #86111 Related #86112 Related #86113 Related #86114 Related #86115 ## Review Repair History The current Phase 1 branch includes focused repairs for ClawSweeper's blocker classes: - Preserves native prefix ownership when `channel-broker` declares platform aliases that overlap native plugins. If Telegram/Discord/Slack are registered, their prefixes stay native-owned; broker aliases own a platform prefix only when no native plugin declares it. - Adds the standard channel SecretRef contract for `channels.channel-broker.{outboundToken,signingSecret}` plus `accounts.*` and `providers.*` variants, and changes the broker schema/docs to use SecretRef-compatible credential input. - Threads `AbortSignal` through broker outbound requests, including the HTTP `fetch` transport and custom runtime override path. ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Verified the Phase 1 broker surface resolves as a bundled channel and produces a normalized V1 outbound request for a Telegram-scoped broker target. This proves the user-visible foundation being shipped in this PR: catalog discovery, channel id normalization, target construction, protocol versioning, platform normalization, thread preservation, and final-send payload shape. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, branch `feat/channel-broker-protocol`, after the current Phase 1 repair/doc series through `d39d47b3db`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash node --import tsx -e 'const { listBundledChannelCatalogEntries } = await import("./src/channels/bundled-channel-catalog-read.ts"); const { normalizeChatChannelId } = await import("./src/channels/ids.ts"); const { buildBrokerConversationTarget, createBrokerOutboundRequest } = await import("./src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts"); const catalog = listBundledChannelCatalogEntries().find((entry) => entry.id === "channel-broker"); const target = buildBrokerConversationTarget({ platform: "Telegram", conversationId: "chat 123", threadId: "topic-7" }); const request = createBrokerOutboundRequest({ requestId: "send-proof-1", providerId: "synthetic-provider", platform: "Telegram", accountId: "default", conversation: { id: "chat 123", type: "channel", threadId: "topic-7" }, mode: "final", payloads: [{ text: "broker proof" }], requirements: { text: true, thread: true } }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ discoveredChannel: catalog && { id: catalog.id, order: catalog.order, docsPath: catalog.channel.docsPath }, channelNormalization: normalizeChatChannelId("channel-broker"), target, outboundProtocolVersion: request.version, outboundMode: request.mode, outboundPlatform: request.platform, outboundConversation: request.conversation, outboundPayloadText: request.payloads[0]?.text }, null, 2));' ``` - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output copied from the command above: ```json { "discoveredChannel": { "id": "channel-broker", "order": 60, "docsPath": "/channels/channel-broker" }, "channelNormalization": "channel-broker", "target": "telegram:chat%20123?threadId=topic-7", "outboundProtocolVersion": 1, "outboundMode": "final", "outboundPlatform": "telegram", "outboundConversation": { "id": "chat 123", "type": "channel", "threadId": "topic-7" }, "outboundPayloadText": "broker proof" } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** OpenClaw discovered the bundled `channel-broker` entry, normalized the channel id, built a Telegram broker target that preserved `threadId`, and emitted a V1 final outbound request with normalized `platform: "telegram"`, `conversation.threadId: "topic-7"`, and payload text `broker proof`. - **What was not tested:** No real third-party broker provider delivery was tested in Phase 1. That is intentionally deferred to the Phase 2 migration proof issues for Telegram, Discord, and Slack. ## Focused Local Validation All local validation below ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: ```bash git diff --check ``` ```bash node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 --config .oxfmtrc.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/runtime.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/types.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.test.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts ``` ## Known Inherited Gates - `pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check` has an inherited clean-upstream SDK budget drift; this PR adds the intended `plugin-sdk/channel-broker` public subpath. - `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` has inherited unrelated glossary-label failures on clean upstream; Channel Broker glossary entries were added in this branch.

target.title: fix: recover Codex binding after stale preflight compaction
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86211.

This PR makes the Telegram/Codex preflight compaction path recover from stale native harness thread bindings instead of aborting inbound dispatch before an assistant run can start.

Changes:
- treats structured stale_thread_binding / missing_thread_binding native harness compaction failures as recoverable during preflight compaction
- keeps non-binding compaction failures fail-closed and visible
- lets Codex binding recovery create a fresh app-server thread when the old binding has already been cleared
- adds focused regression coverage for both the preflight dispatch path and the cleared-binding Codex recovery path

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Inbound Telegram/Codex dispatch no longer aborts when preflight compaction sees a stale or missing Codex app-server thread binding. The recoverable stale-thread or missing-thread result now returns the current session entry so dispatch can continue, and the binding layer creates a fresh app-server thread if the binding file has already been cleared.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at /root/.openclaw/workspace/openclaw-upstream on Linux with Node 22.22.2, branch fix-86211-missing-thread-preflight, commit 4b8ff8685fd9d4d3bcd901c30abc76ce9c98dd34, rebased on current origin/main.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran targeted OpenClaw runtime checks in the local checkout: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts; node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts; node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts; node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts; focused npx oxlint on the changed core and extension files.

Evidence after fix: Local terminal output showed agent-runner-memory.test.ts passed with 27 tests, conversation-binding.test.ts passed with 13 tests, compact.test.ts passed with 26 tests, cli-compaction.test.ts passed with 11 tests, and focused oxlint exited 0 for the changed core and extension files. The command output was captured after rebasing on origin/main; PR text uses only redacted placeholders such as <codex-thread-id> and no chat IDs.

Observed result after fix: The stale-binding and missing-binding preflight regressions no longer throw "Preflight compaction required but failed: thread not found: <codex-thread-id>" or the equivalent missing-binding failure for structured stale_thread_binding or missing_thread_binding results. Non-binding failures still throw. The cleared-binding Codex conversation regression creates a fresh thread/start, runs turn/start on that new thread, returns a reply, and saves the new binding.

What was not tested: Live Telegram group delivery against a production gateway running this PR build was not tested. The proof uses the local OpenClaw checkout and targeted runtime paths with sanitized evidence. I requested the repository Mantis Telegram proof path in PR comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86216#issuecomment-4530350483, but direct workflow dispatch requires maintainer/admin rights.

## Validation

Passed locally:
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts
- npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
- npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts

Also ran node scripts/check-changed.mjs. Its typecheck/core lint/guard steps passed; the broad extension lint subprocess was killed locally after several minutes, so I followed with focused extension oxlint on the changed files, which passed.

## OpenClaw proof gate status

The repository OpenClaw `Real behavior proof` workflow has now re-evaluated the current PR head and passed.

- Head: `4b8ff8685fd9d4d3bcd901c30abc76ce9c98dd34`
- Run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26375423624
- Job log result: `External PR includes after-fix real behavior proof.`

This means the remaining status is no longer a contributor-side Mantis dispatch blocker. The earlier Mantis request is kept only as historical context for the previous proof suggestion; it is not the active unblock path.

A reviewer can now re-check the current PR state against the passing OpenClaw proof gate and the focused validation above.

## Privacy

All issue and PR evidence is sanitized. Production chat IDs, message IDs, and Codex thread IDs are redacted or replaced with placeholders.
