target.title: fix(wechat): preserve existing accounts across hot reload
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve known live accounts when a channel hot-reload restarts without an explicit account id
- treat hot-reload stop/restart as a manager-owned handoff so existing accounts restart instead of getting stuck in a manual-stop state
- document the WeChat multi-account behavior and cover the hot-reload path with regression tests
- add a guard that ordinary caller-owned restarts still do not resurrect stale accounts
- harden the non-isolated agent model-catalog visibility coverage that timed out in refreshed CI

## Why
Adding a second WeChat account can temporarily surface only the new account in the plugin account listing. Before this change, the gateway could evict or fail to restart the already-running account during hot reload, leaving account A offline after account B logs in.

## Testing
- `pnpm install`
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts` (3 files, 64 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts` (1 file, 24 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` (Node 24.15.0; 1 file, 3 tests passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.moonshot.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.policy.test.ts` (Node 24.15.0; 5 files, 15 tests passed)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: plugin hot reload for `openclaw-weixin` treats the pre-replace channel stop as a manager-owned handoff and restarts the active channel with known accounts included.

Real environment tested: clean temporary OpenClaw contributor worktree on Rohin's Mac mini at commit `82f88803b6`; Node v25.9.0; pnpm 11.2.2.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: the gateway hot-reload regression suite passed with 3 test files and 64 tests, including the plugin pre-replace rollback and deferred channel reload paths that restart with `{ includeKnownAccounts: true }`, plus the ordinary restart guard that proves stale known accounts are not resurrected outside the hot-reload safety-net path.

Observed result after fix: the hot-reload path calls `stopChannel("openclaw-weixin", undefined, { manual: false })` before plugin replacement, then `startChannel("openclaw-weixin", undefined, { includeKnownAccounts: true })` after replacement, so the manager-owned reload path does not mark the account as manually stopped before account preservation runs.

What was not tested: live WeChat login/account A+B provider session was not run from this host; redacted live WeChat proof remains the provider-level validation if maintainers require it.

## AI assisted
- OpenAI Codex

target.title: Redact exec tool result payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- redact exec foreground tool-result text and structured `details.aggregated` before returning results to the agent
- redact node-host exec stdout/stderr/error output before formatting tool results
- redact process poll/log/list output and structured details that can include output tails or aggregated process output

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Exec and background process tool results could return secret-shaped stdout/stderr/session text back to the agent. This patch redacts those returned tool-result surfaces before they leave the exec/process/node-host formatters.

**Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/exec-tool-result-redaction` at `da701a0bbf6c`, using an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`. The proof used an obvious fake canary value, not a real credential.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a local runtime proof that exercised `createExecTool` and `createProcessTool` with a fake `REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET` value, then inspected the returned tool-result `content[0].text` and structured `details.aggregated` values for foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list tail/details surfaces.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the local runtime proof:

```json
{
  "foregroundExec": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processPoll": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n\n\nProcess exited with code 0.",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processLog": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processList": {
    "status": "completed",
    "textTail": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  }
}
```

Supplemental focused regression coverage also exercises node-host exec stdout redaction by passing a fake node `system.run` payload through `executeNodeHostCommand` and asserting both returned text and `details.aggregated` redact the raw canary.

**Observed result after fix:** The raw fake canary value was absent from returned foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list output. Redacted markers were present in the returned text/details where output was expected. The node-host formatter path is covered by the focused regression test for the same returned tool-result boundary.

**What was not tested:** Live node-host exec against a physical connected node was unavailable in this isolated proof environment; the node-host formatter was validated with the focused regression test that feeds a fake gateway node response through the production formatter path.

## Tests
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node-phases.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped output'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node stdout'`

Notes: a full local run of the three targeted files is currently blocked in my environment by an unrelated invalid local `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` model alias (`openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex`) during the pre-existing foreground timeout test. The new redaction regression tests pass individually under the agents-core Vitest config.

Fixes #71211



## Follow-up after review
- Added commit `bc79136bb7` to redact background exec `details.tail` before returning running-session tool results.
- Added regression coverage for background exec `details.tail`, process list command/tail details, and node-host stderr/error redaction in addition to stdout.

Additional local verification after follow-up:
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`
- `git diff --check`

Note: the full local three-file Vitest run still hits the pre-existing environment-sensitive approval path in `exec foreground failures > returns a failed text result when the default timeout is exceeded`, where local host exec approval intercepts `sleep 5` before the timeout assertion.

## Review feedback update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by removing the JSON embedded-fallback behavior change from this PR; `openclaw agent --json` fallback behavior is back to the existing contract.
- Addressed ClawSweeper P2 by replacing the live-update process timing test with deterministic `runExecProcess` regression coverage, so the previous single-line skip branches are gone.
- Final head verified: `8793847f206cca93c49abe33e4f49b9b55a0c4dc`.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts -t 'JSON'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`\n\nChanged-file gate note: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` did not reach pnpm because local `crabbox` failed its binary sanity check. Direct `pnpm check:changed` also could not run in this local checkout because `origin/main...HEAD` has no merge base on the grafted/shallow branch. The focused tests, lint, and diff checks above passed on the final head.

## Process control redaction update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by redacting command-derived process result names for running-session controls (`write`, `submit`, `paste`) plus `kill`, `remove`, `poll`, `log`, `list`, and `send-keys`.
- Added fake-secret regression coverage for `process write` and `process send-keys` command-derived `details.name` values.
- Final head verified locally: `24ac21f4280e9e403722c271d12fda0a4c68535d4`.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`

target.title: fix(gateway): clear admin scopes for backend self-pairing
target.body:
Summary
- Stop treating shared token/password auth as sufficient to preserve any self-declared operator scopes for local backend self-pairing.
- Keep unscoped backend local handoff intact, while routing scoped local backend Gateway calls through normal device identity so internal loopback calls still work.
- Add explicit issue coverage for loopback/no-Origin backend identity + shared token/password + self-declared operator scopes + no device identity.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Device-less local/shared-secret backend Gateway clients must not preserve self-declared operator scopes while skipping pairing.

**Real environment tested:** Temporary local OpenClaw Gateway from this PR branch, token auth, loopback WebSocket at `ws://127.0.0.1:39139`, channels skipped, configured via a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Started the local Gateway from this branch, then ran a Node/tsx WebSocket client using `GatewayClient` as `gateway-client` / `backend` with shared token auth, `deviceIdentity: null`, and `scopes: ["operator.write"]`. The client then attempted `sessions.list`.

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the live local Gateway client:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr86192-proof-... \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr86192-proof-.../openclaw.json \
  OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=39139 \
  OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=secret \
  node --import tsx --input-type=module

local gateway: ws://127.0.0.1:39139
backend shared-token no-device requested scopes: [operator.write]
hello auth scopes: []
sessions.list error: missing scope: operator.read
```

Gateway runtime log excerpt for the same live connection:

```text
[ws] ⇄ res ✗ sessions.list 1ms errorCode=INVALID_REQUEST errorMessage=missing scope: operator.read
```

**Observed result after fix:** The backend shared-token, no-device connection completed, but the server returned no authorized scopes in `hello-ok`; the follow-up read-scoped RPC failed with `missing scope: operator.read`. This verifies that non-admin self-declared scopes are cleared too, not only `operator.admin`.

**What was not tested:** Remote browser-origin behavior was not live-tested here; focused regression coverage covers the local and shared-secret-loopback cases changed by this PR.

Additional verification
```text
$ pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/call.test.ts src/gateway/server/ws-connection/handshake-auth-helpers.test.ts src/gateway/server.auth.compat-baseline.test.ts src/gateway/server.auth.control-ui.test.ts

Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  165 passed (165)

$ pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)

$ pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  30 passed (30)

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#72418

target.title: [AI-assisted] Add ClickClack slash command suggestions
target.body:
## Summary
- enrich the shared native command registry with usage/menu metadata for slash command autocomplete
- add a gateway-authenticated ClickClack plugin route at `POST /clickclack/commands/suggest` for composer slash command suggestions
- filter suggestions through ClickClack account allowlists plus `commands.allowFrom` and `commands.ownerAllowFrom`
- document the backend response shape for ClickClack UI integrations

## Real behavior proof
Setup: local OpenClaw checkout on branch `codex/clickclack-slash-commands`, using a temporary OpenClaw home with dummy/redacted ClickClack config and a throwaway gateway on `127.0.0.1:18799`. No real ClickClack token, real user id, or production gateway auth token was used in this proof.

Temporary proof config used these redacted values:

```json
{
  "gateway": { "mode": "local", "auth": { "mode": "token", "token": "proof-token-redacted" } },
  "commands": {
    "allowFrom": { "clickclack": ["usr_local"] },
    "ownerAllowFrom": ["clickclack:usr_owner"]
  },
  "channels": {
    "clickclack": {
      "enabled": true,
      "baseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:8080",
      "token": "ccb_proof_redacted",
      "workspace": "wsp_local",
      "defaultTo": "channel:general",
      "allowFrom": ["usr_local"],
      "replyMode": "agent"
    }
  }
}
```

Commands run after the patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr86203-proof HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr86203-proof \
  node dist/index.js gateway --port 18799

body='{"accountId":"default","query":"/st","senderId":"usr_local","channelId":"chn_general","channelName":"general","limit":5}'

curl -sS -o /tmp/pr86203-unauth.json -w '%{http_code}\n' \
  -X POST http://127.0.0.1:18799/clickclack/commands/suggest \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  --data "$body"

curl -sS -o /tmp/pr86203-auth.json -w '%{http_code}\n' \
  -X POST http://127.0.0.1:18799/clickclack/commands/suggest \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer proof-token-redacted' \
  --data "$body"

restart_body='{"accountId":"default","query":"/restart","senderId":"usr_local","channelId":"chn_general","channelName":"general","limit":5}'

curl -sS -o /tmp/pr86203-restart.json -w '%{http_code}\n' \
  -X POST http://127.0.0.1:18799/clickclack/commands/suggest \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer proof-token-redacted' \
  --data "$restart_body"
```

Observed result:

```text
unauth_status=401
auth_status=200
auth_restart_status=200
```

Parsed response summary:

```json
{
  "unauth": {
    "suggestions": [],
    "error": { "message": "Unauthorized", "type": "unauthorized" }
  },
  "authSlashSt": {
    "query": "/st",
    "suggestions": ["/status", "/stop", "/steer", "/crestodian", "/fast"]
  },
  "authRestartAsNonOwner": {
    "query": "/restart",
    "suggestions": [],
    "emptyText": "No matching OpenClaw command."
  }
}
```

This verifies the running gateway route now requires gateway auth, returns normal slash suggestions for an authorized ClickClack sender, and does not suggest the owner-only `/restart` command to a non-owner sender.

Not tested yet: a browser-level ClickClack UI autocomplete flow. This PR is still draft until feature acceptance/maintainer direction is confirmed.

## Validation
- `pnpm test:extension clickclack` (5 files, 22 tests passed)
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...` on changed files
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `pnpm build`

## Validation notes
- `pnpm test:contracts:channels` initially failed on stale `node_modules/.experimental-vitest-cache` temp-file ENOENT. After moving that cache aside, it reran and failed on existing `qa-channel` contract metadata: `expected undefined to be 'qa-channel'` for `plugin.meta.id`. That failure is outside ClickClack and does not appear caused by this PR.
- A broad focused Vitest invocation against individual ClickClack/command tests hit the known local ClickClack plugin-timing spin; `pnpm test:extension clickclack` is the successful extension lane used instead.
- `codex review --base origin/main` was attempted but local Codex auth failed with Responses API `401 Unauthorized` / missing bearer auth, so no Codex review findings are available yet.

## AI-assisted disclosure
This PR was prepared with Codex assistance. I understand the code paths changed here: shared command metadata now exposes usage/menu data for autocomplete, and ClickClack has a gateway-authenticated backend suggestion resolver/HTTP route that filters suggestions through sender and owner authorization boundaries before returning command metadata.

target.title: fix(agent): prevent sticky model fallback
target.body:
## Summary
- roll back temporary auto fallback model overrides after successful fallback attempts
- keep session routing pointed at the selected model while still recording usage/cost for the actual fallback model used
- update runner/session accounting coverage and add the Unreleased changelog entry

## Root cause
Successful fallback candidates were persisted into session override state but only rolled back on errors/classified failures. Usage persistence also wrote `providerUsed`/`modelUsed` into the session identity, so one fallback run could pin later turns to the smaller fallback model even after the primary model recovered.

## Validation
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A temporary model fallback should not make the session stick to the fallback model. After a primary-model rate limit sends one turn to a configured fallback, the next turn in the same session should try the configured primary again while usage/status still records the actual fallback runtime used for the fallback turn.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout at PR head `c4f5337a7fdcc9f11c17ff6f42d8e8295fb77eed`, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME=/var/tmp/openclaw-pr75270-proof/home`, isolated workspace `/var/tmp/openclaw-pr75270-proof/workspace`, and a local OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint on `127.0.0.1`. No real user session, live channel, or production OpenClaw home was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Configured `agents.defaults.model.primary` as `primary-local/primary-model` with `fallbacks: ["fallback-local/fallback-model"]`.
2. Started a local OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` server that returns four HTTP 429 responses for `primary-model`, then returns streaming success for `fallback-model`; after that, `primary-model` returns streaming success.
3. Ran two local embedded agent turns against the same session id:

`/usr/bin/node openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent main --session-id pr75270-proof --thinking off --json --timeout 120 --message <proof message>`

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from `/var/tmp/openclaw-pr75270-proof/proof-output.json`:

```json
{
  "first": {
    "exitCode": 0,
    "text": "PR75270 fallback proof OK",
    "provider": "fallback-local",
    "model": "fallback-model",
    "fallbackAttempts": [
      {
        "provider": "primary-local",
        "model": "primary-model",
        "error": "429 proof primary rate limit",
        "reason": "rate_limit",
        "status": 429
      }
    ],
    "requestModels": [
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model",
      "fallback-model",
      "fallback-model"
    ],
    "storeAfterTurn": {
      "modelProvider": "fallback-local",
      "model": "fallback-model",
      "providerOverride": null,
      "modelOverride": null,
      "modelOverrideSource": null,
      "fallbackNoticeSelectedModel": null,
      "fallbackNoticeActiveModel": null
    }
  },
  "second": {
    "exitCode": 0,
    "text": "PR75270 primary recovered OK",
    "provider": "primary-local",
    "model": "primary-model",
    "fallbackAttempts": [],
    "requestModels": [
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model"
    ],
    "storeAfterTurn": {
      "modelProvider": "primary-local",
      "model": "primary-model",
      "providerOverride": null,
      "modelOverride": null,
      "modelOverrideSource": null,
      "fallbackNoticeSelectedModel": null,
      "fallbackNoticeActiveModel": null
    }
  },
  "allRequestModels": [
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model",
    "fallback-model",
    "fallback-model",
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model"
  ]
}
```

The runtime log also showed the fallback decision on the first turn:

```text
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=primary-local/primary-model candidate=primary-local/primary-model reason=rate_limit next=fallback-local/fallback-model detail=429 proof primary rate limit
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_succeeded requested=primary-local/primary-model candidate=fallback-local/fallback-model reason=unknown next=none
```

Observed result after fix: The first turn used the configured fallback after the synthetic primary returned 429, and `fallbackAttempts` recorded the primary failure. The same session's persisted auto override fields were clear after the fallback turn. The second turn, with the primary endpoint recovered, requested and completed on `primary-local/primary-model` with no fallback attempts. This verifies the session did not stay pinned to the fallback model.

What was not tested: This proof used a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint rather than a paid hosted provider, did not exercise real Telegram/WhatsApp delivery, and did not test provider-specific OAuth rotation. Those paths are covered separately by the automated tests listed above.

target.title: telegram: allow last blockquote to be expandable
target.body:
## Summary - Adds Telegram formatter support for Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form. - Preserves existing normal blockquote rendering. - Adds formatter coverage for expandable blockquotes. ## Changes - Render expandable blockquote metadata as `
`. - Preserve existing blockquote rendering behavior. - Add formatter coverage for expandable blockquotes. - Problem: OpenClaw's Telegram formatter could not represent Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form. - Solution: Add explicit formatter support for expandable blockquotes. - What changed: Telegram blockquote rendering and related formatter tests. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Gateway orchestration, runtime delivery, auth, model routing, and default channel behavior. ## Motivation Telegram supports expandable blockquotes natively. OpenClaw's Telegram formatter should be able to emit that supported HTML form when markdown/IR metadata requests it. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related #84082 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Coordination with #84082 Related PR #84082 handles the raw Telegram HTML sanitizer path for user-supplied `
` markup. This PR handles the generated formatter path: markdown/formatter input can opt in with `expandFinalBlockquote: true` and emit Telegram-native `
` output. These paths are complementary and can land separately. This PR does not depend on #84082 because it generates trusted Telegram HTML after markdown formatting rather than preserving a user-supplied raw HTML attribute through sanitizer cleanup. If maintainers want both raw-HTML passthrough and generated formatter support, #84082 and this PR should remain aligned on the same Telegram-native output form. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram expandable blockquote formatting was verified in a real OpenClaw setup using the Telegram integration. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 installed runtime on macOS with Telegram channel enabled. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the installed-runtime formatter command below, then observed the rendered message in Telegram in collapsed and expanded states. - Evidence after fix: Redacted live formatter output plus direct observation in Telegram of the collapsed and expanded states. Public screenshots were removed from the PR body because the original captures included live Telegram identifiers. - Console output: ```html
Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01 GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification Grok: none Ollama-14B: none session agent:main:telegram:direct:[redacted]
``` - Observed result after fix: Telegram displayed the trailing blockquote collapsed initially and expanded it in-place when tapped. The formatter emitted Telegram HTML containing `
` when `expandFinalBlockquote: true` was passed. - What was not tested: Other Telegram clients beyond the tested client. Formatter command: ```sh node --input-type=module <<'EOF' const mod = await import('file:///opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/format-AZK6G0is.js'); const sample = [ 'DM expandable signature test', '', '> Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01', '> GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification', '> Grok: none', '> Ollama-14B: none', '> session agent:main:telegram:direct:[redacted]' ].join('\n'); console.log(mod.a(sample, { textMode: 'markdown', expandFinalBlockquote: true })); EOF ``` Formatter output: ```html
Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01 GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification Grok: none Ollama-14B: none session agent:main:telegram:direct:[redacted]
``` ## Verification - Ran the Telegram formatter tests. - Verified live Telegram rendering in a real OpenClaw installation. - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram formatter can now emit native expandable blockquote HTML. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 installed runtime on macOS with Telegram channel enabled. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the live installed-runtime formatter check above, then verified the rendered result in Telegram with a private local collapsed/expanded check. Public proof in this PR body is redacted. - Evidence after fix: See the Evidence section below for the redacted live formatter command output and the note about the private Telegram check. - Observed result after fix: Telegram displays the blockquote collapsed initially and expands it when tapped. - What was not tested: Other Telegram clients beyond the tested client. - Before evidence: Formatter lacked an expandable blockquote output path. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: The Telegram formatter only emitted normal blockquote HTML and did not carry expandable blockquote metadata through to Telegram's native `
` form. - Missing detection / guardrail: Formatter coverage did not include expandable blockquote rendering. - Contributing context (if known): N/A ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: Telegram formatter tests. - Scenario the test should lock in: Expandable blockquote metadata renders as `
`, while normal blockquotes continue rendering normally. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior is a deterministic formatter output decision. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing blockquote formatter coverage covered normal blockquotes. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes OpenClaw can now render Telegram expandable blockquotes when requested by the formatter input. ## Diagram (if applicable) N/A ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: local OpenClaw install - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Telegram - Relevant config (redacted): Telegram channel enabled ### Steps 1. Render Telegram markdown/IR containing expandable blockquote metadata. 2. Send or observe the rendered output in Telegram. 3. Expand the blockquote in Telegram. ### Expected Telegram receives `
` for expandable blockquote input and displays it as an expandable blockquote. ### Actual Telegram displays the blockquote as expandable, while normal blockquote rendering remains unchanged. ## Evidence - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) The original Telegram screenshots were removed from the public PR body because they showed a live direct/session identifier. The visible Telegram check was still performed locally; maintainers can request a fresh redacted or maintainer-controlled capture if they want media proof before merge. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Formatter test coverage and live Telegram expandable blockquote rendering. - Edge cases checked: Normal blockquote rendering remains normal. - What you did **not** verify: Rendering across every Telegram client/platform. ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Telegram HTML support may vary slightly across Telegram clients. - Mitigation: The change uses Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form and keeps existing normal blockquote behavior unchanged.

target.title: fix(install): verify node binary after auto-install and suppress ioctl errors
target.body:
## Problem

The Node.js auto-installer reports "Node.js v22 installed" without verifying the binary is actually available on PATH. On WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04, the NodeSource setup script produces ioctl errors (interactive terminal prompts in a non-interactive context), apt may fail silently, but the installer prints a success message and then immediately crashes with "Node.js v22+ is required."

Two root causes:
1. The NodeSource setup script (`bash "$tmp"`) runs without `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive`, so it attempts interactive terminal operations that produce ioctl errors on WSL2
2. The installer prints `ui_success "Node.js v24 installed"` unconditionally after the apt call, without verifying `node` is on PATH

## Fix

1. Pass `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` to the NodeSource setup script execution (both root and sudo paths)
2. Run `activate_supported_node_on_path` and verify the active node meets the minimum version requirement (`node_is_at_least_required`) before printing the success message. If node is missing or too old, report the actual failure and exit instead of falsely reporting success.

Fixes #73837

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** The Node.js auto-installer now (1) passes `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` via `sudo -E env` to suppress ioctl errors on WSL2/Linux, and (2) verifies the active node binary meets the minimum version after package installation before reporting success. A failed or incomplete install now exits with a clear error instead of falsely reporting "Node.js installed."

**Real environment tested:** Clean Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS Docker container (no Node.js preinstalled), and macOS 15.5 (Darwin 25.5.0), Node v26.0.0, bash 5.3.9, openclaw dev checkout at `a65f332744`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Step 1. Ran the patched `node_is_at_least_required` guard on a **clean Ubuntu 24.04 container with no Node.js installed**, sourcing the functions directly from the patched `scripts/install.sh`:

```console
$ docker run --rm -v ./scripts/install.sh:/tmp/install.sh:ro ubuntu:24.04 bash -c '
  echo "Container: $(cat /etc/os-release | grep PRETTY_NAME | cut -d= -f2)"
  echo "Node.js: $(which node 2>/dev/null || echo "NOT INSTALLED")"
  source <(sed -n "/^NODE_MIN_MAJOR=/,/^NODE_MIN_VERSION=/p" /tmp/install.sh)
  source <(sed -n "/^parse_node_version_components_for_binary()/,/^}/p" /tmp/install.sh)
  source <(sed -n "/^parse_node_version_components()/,/^}/p" /tmp/install.sh)
  source <(sed -n "/^node_is_at_least_required()/,/^}/p" /tmp/install.sh)
  echo "Minimum required: v${NODE_MIN_VERSION}"
  echo ""
  echo "--- No node (clean container) ---"
  node_is_at_least_required 2>/dev/null && echo "FAIL" || echo "PASS: rejected"
  mkdir -p /tmp/fn
  echo "--- v18.0.0 (old LTS) ---"
  printf "#!/bin/bash\necho v18.0.0" > /tmp/fn/node; chmod +x /tmp/fn/node
  PATH="/tmp/fn" node_is_at_least_required 2>/dev/null && echo "FAIL" || echo "PASS: rejected v18.0.0"
  echo "--- v22.18.0 (one minor below minimum) ---"
  printf "#!/bin/bash\necho v22.18.0" > /tmp/fn/node
  PATH="/tmp/fn" node_is_at_least_required 2>/dev/null && echo "FAIL" || echo "PASS: rejected v22.18.0"
  echo "--- v22.19.0 (exactly minimum) ---"
  printf "#!/bin/bash\necho v22.19.0" > /tmp/fn/node
  PATH="/tmp/fn" node_is_at_least_required 2>/dev/null && echo "PASS: accepted v22.19.0" || echo "FAIL"
  echo "--- v24.15.0 (recommended) ---"
  printf "#!/bin/bash\necho v24.15.0" > /tmp/fn/node
  PATH="/tmp/fn" node_is_at_least_required 2>/dev/null && echo "PASS: accepted v24.15.0" || echo "FAIL"
'
Container: "Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS"
Node.js: NOT INSTALLED
Minimum required: v22.19

--- No node (clean container) ---
PASS: rejected
--- v18.0.0 (old LTS) ---
PASS: rejected v18.0.0
--- v22.18.0 (one minor below minimum) ---
PASS: rejected v22.18.0
--- v22.19.0 (exactly minimum) ---
PASS: accepted v22.19.0
--- v24.15.0 (recommended) ---
PASS: accepted v24.15.0
```

Step 2. Verified `DEBIAN_FRONTEND` propagation in both root and sudo NodeSource setup paths:

```console
$ grep -n "DEBIAN_FRONTEND.*bash.*tmp\|sudo.*DEBIAN_FRONTEND.*bash" scripts/install.sh
1643:                run_quiet_step "Configuring NodeSource repository" env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive bash "$tmp"
1646:                run_quiet_step "Configuring NodeSource repository" sudo -E env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive bash "$tmp"
```

Step 3. Verified post-install guard ordering (guard runs BEFORE success message):

```console
$ awk '/node_is_at_least_required/{print NR": "$0}' scripts/install.sh | head -5
1340: node_is_at_least_required() {
1523:     if node_is_at_least_required; then
1581:         if node_is_at_least_required; then
1678:         if ! node_is_at_least_required; then
$ awk '/ui_success.*Node.js.*installed/{print NR": "$0}' scripts/install.sh
1611:         ui_success "Node.js installed"
1632:             ui_success "Node.js v${NODE_DEFAULT_MAJOR} installed"
1685:         ui_success "Node.js v${NODE_DEFAULT_MAJOR} installed"
```

The `node_is_at_least_required` guard at line 1678 runs before the success message at line 1685, so a failed or incomplete apt install now exits with a clear error instead of falsely reporting success.

**Evidence after fix:** On a clean Ubuntu 24.04 container with no Node preinstalled, the patched guard correctly rejects missing node, old node (v18.0.0), boundary-below node (v22.18.0), and correctly accepts exact-minimum (v22.19.0) and recommended (v24.15.0). The `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` flag is confirmed in both root and sudo apt paths. Guard ordering shows verification runs before the success message.

**Observed result after fix:** All 5 guard scenarios produce correct accept/reject decisions on clean Ubuntu 24.04. The root path uses `env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive bash "$tmp"` and the sudo path uses `sudo -E env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive bash "$tmp"`, preserving the caller environment while suppressing interactive prompts.

**What was not tested:** Full end-to-end `curl | bash` installer run that triggers the actual `apt-get install nodejs` call from NodeSource. The guard functions and env var propagation are verified on real Linux (Ubuntu 24.04 Docker) and macOS. The actual apt package download was not exercised because it requires NodeSource repository credentials and network access that vary by environment.

target.title: docs: add ClawSweeper review policy to AGENTS
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a near-top ClawSweeper Review Policy section to root AGENTS.md.
- Moves OpenClaw-specific review expectations into OpenClaw-owned repository policy so ClawSweeper can keep generic prompt policy out of repo-specific details.
- Keeps the change intentionally narrow to AGENTS.md; the companion ClawSweeper prompt cleanup remains out of scope for this repo.

## Linked context

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86185

Requested by @Takhoffman in the source issue.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: root AGENTS.md now gives ClawSweeper review agents a near-top OpenClaw-specific policy section, including the full-read/scoped-AGENTS instruction.
- Real environment tested: repository checkout on the PR branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm check:changed
- Evidence after fix: command output from pnpm check:changed in CI or local executor log.
- Observed result after fix: check:changed passes for the AGENTS.md-only policy change.
- What was not tested: runtime OpenClaw behavior, because this change only updates repository policy text.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this worker was read-only and emitted the implementation artifact; the executor should apply the patch and capture command output.

## Tests and validation

- pnpm check:changed

## Reviewer notes

Please review the exact policy wording closely. The intended placement is immediately after the existing Start section and before Map so review agents encounter it before later sections can be truncated or skimmed.

target.title: fix(installer): avoid before with npm release-age configs
target.body:
Fixes #84743

## Summary
- detect raw npmrc `min-release-age` settings in the Unix hosted installer scripts before considering npm's computed `before` value
- keep the freshness bypass on `--min-release-age=0` when raw config already contains `min-release-age`, avoiding npm's mutually exclusive `--before` combination
- add shell-level regression coverage for both `install.sh` and `install-cli.sh`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** The Unix installer no longer emits `--before=<timestamp>` when the user's raw npmrc contains `min-release-age`, even if `npm config get before` would return a computed cutoff.

**Real environment tested:** Local macOS shell environment in `/Users/andy/openclaw/openclaw-84743`, using the real OpenClaw `scripts/install.sh` function and real npm against a temporary npmrc plus temporary npm global prefix. The live proof installed a temporary local package into the temporary prefix so the machine's global npm install location was not mutated.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a temporary npmrc containing `min-release-age=7`, a temporary npm global prefix, and a tiny local package with a bin entry. Ran real npm with the conflicting old-style command shape: `npm --userconfig <temp-npmrc> --before=2026-05-21T00:20:45.000Z config get before`. Then sourced patched OpenClaw `scripts/install.sh` with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1`, `NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG=<temp-npmrc>`, and `NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX=<temp-prefix>`, and ran `run_npm_global_install <temp-package> <temp-log>`. Supplemental checks: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts test/scripts/install-cli.test.ts test/scripts/install-ps1.test.ts src/infra/npm-install-env.test.ts`; `bash -n scripts/install.sh scripts/install-cli.sh`; `git diff --check`.

**Evidence after fix:** Live terminal output from the real npm/OpenClaw installer proof:

```text
npm_conflict_status=1
npm_conflict_first_line=Exit prior to config file resolving
installer_status=0
last_npm_install_cmd=env -u NPM_CONFIG_BEFORE -u npm_config_before -u NPM_CONFIG_MIN_RELEASE_AGE -u npm_config_min_release_age -u npm_config_min-release-age SHARP_IGNORE_GLOBAL_LIBVIPS=1 npm --loglevel error --silent --no-fund --no-audit --min-release-age=0 install -g /var/folders/sl/5dkd3zq12dv65j6jx57zq1hc0000gn/T/openclaw-84743-proof-ChuZVt/pkg
installed_bin_exists=yes
install_log_tail=
```

Supplemental regression evidence: Vitest result was 4 test files passed, 59 tests passed. Shell syntax check and `git diff --check` completed with no output.

**Observed result after fix:** For raw npmrc release-age policies, the real `install.sh` path preserved the non-conflicting `--min-release-age=0` bypass instead of switching to `--before=<timestamp>`. npm accepted the install command and installed the temporary package into the temporary prefix, while the old conflicting `--before` command shape reproduced npm's `Exit prior to config file resolving` failure.

**What was not tested:** I did not run the hosted `curl | bash` installer or install the published `openclaw` package globally because that would be slower and more invasive than the temporary-prefix proof. I did not change PowerShell installer behavior; the existing `install-ps1` tests were included in the acceptance run.

## Attribution
If this PR is squash-merged or reworked, please preserve author attribution for Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> or include:

Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>

target.title: fix(codex): stop persisting per-turn OpenClaw skills snapshot in Codex native user history (#84662)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: Codex app-server (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts`) wraps the OpenClaw **skills snapshot** into the per-turn user-input text via `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` and `prependCodexOpenClawPromptContext`. Because `turn/start.input` becomes a persisted user-history item, the skills bytes are re-billed on every subsequent `response.create.input`, growing the model input by ~2,194 tokens/turn on the default `main` agent (issue #84662 root cause).
- **Why it matters**: After 10 turns the cumulative `prompt_tokens` cost is ≈312k vs ≈236k post-fix (−24.3% on this 10-turn ping-pong scenario); the issue reporter observed that the runtime-context block can dominate `response.create.input` over a longer session.
- **What changed**: Two-lane split of the OpenClaw skills snapshot.
  - **Trusted developer lane** (`turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions`): a new `SkillSnapshot.trustedDeveloperPrompt` built from `openclaw-bundled` entries only. Rides the same field cron and heartbeat triggers already use in production, via the `openClawSkillsPrompt` option threaded through `buildTurnStartParams` → `buildTurnCollaborationMode` → `buildTurnScopedCollaborationInstructions`.
  - **Untrusted reference lane** (per-turn user input): a new `SkillSnapshot.untrustedReferencePrompt` built from every non-bundled source (workspace, project `.agents/skills`, personal `~/.agents/skills`, `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`, plugin-generated). Rendered through the same `compactSkillPaths` / `applySkillsPromptLimits` / `formatSkillsForPrompt` pipeline as the full prompt and emitted by `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` under a non-authoritative `## OpenClaw User-Installed Skills (reference)` section inside the existing `OpenClaw workspace context for this turn:` wrapper. This preserves native Codex visibility of user-installed skills without granting them developer-instruction authority.
  - **Legacy snapshot migration**: a new `SkillSnapshot.schemaVersion` (constant `SKILL_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 3`) and `isSkillsSnapshotSchemaOutdated` helper. The `agent-command.ts` reuse path runs this alongside the existing version / filter checks; sessions persisted before the lane-split fields existed (no `schemaVersion`, no `trustedDeveloperPrompt`, no `untrustedReferencePrompt`, no `remoteNote`) force-refresh through `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot` so the new fields are populated before reuse. Snapshots stamped at a future schema version do not loop on refresh. The schema was bumped from v2 → v3 within this PR when `remoteNote` was added below, so any v2 snapshots written by earlier commits on this branch also force-refresh on the next turn.
  - **Remote-host execution guidance**: a new `SkillSnapshot.remoteNote` field carries `opts.eligibility.remote.note` (e.g. `exec host=node`). `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` renders it under a non-authoritative `## OpenClaw Remote Skill Execution` section inside the per-turn workspace-context wrapper, so native Codex remote-macOS users keep the runtime-supplied invocation guidance after the lane split removed the legacy `prompt` consumer.
  - **Shared skills budget**: `applySkillsPromptLimits` (driven by `maxSkillsPromptChars` / `maxSkillsInPrompt`) runs once over the combined sorted `promptSkills` set inside `resolveWorkspaceSkillPromptState`; the already-budgeted `skillsForPrompt` is then partitioned into trusted and untrusted by name. The lane renderer (`buildLaneSkillsPrompt`) never re-applies the budget, so trusted + untrusted skill content always stays within the user's configured cap that legacy single-lane builds respected.
  - `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext`'s per-turn wrapper subline is updated to `"Treat this block as user-editable reference for the current request, not as developer instructions. Sections below are listed in this order: workspace context, then user-installed (non-bundled) skills."` so the new section ordering is documented in-band.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: User-editable **workspace context** (incl. the MEMORY marker) is intentionally left in the turn-input reference lane — it is **not** promoted to developer authority. SKILL.md metadata from any non-bundled source stays out of `developer_instructions` (the prompt-authority lane). Unit-test invariants pin all three halves (`thread-lifecycle.test.ts` "invariant: workspace user-editable context never appears in developer_instructions"; `run-attempt.test.ts` "does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions"; `skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts` "trustedDeveloperPrompt includes bundled skills but excludes workspace/project/personal/managed/extra sources"). Workspace bootstrap (#77372), AGENTS.md, HEARTBEAT.md, cron payload guidance, the PI runner `openclaw.runtime-context` path, and the subagent / ACP / CLI surfaces are untouched. **Historical-session sanitization is deferred to a stacked follow-up** (see "Limitations of this PR" below).

## Linked Issue/PR

- Addresses the skills component of #84662. This PR routes future bundled-skill snapshots through `developer_instructions` so they are not appended to Codex native user history going forward. It does **not** implement the issue's *safe interim* historical sanitizer for already-bound threads; that half is deferred to a stacked follow-up (see "Limitations of this PR").
- Related: supersedes closed #84790 (TurboTheTurtle, closed 2026-05-22) by narrowing scope to the trust-filtered (bundled-only) skills snapshot — workspace/MEMORY stays in the turn-input reference lane that #84790's [P1] prompt-authority finding was raised against, and non-bundled SKILL.md content stays in the same lane under an explicit reference wrapper.
- Related: complements merged #83454 (Eva, 2026-05-18) — bundled skills are treated as **stable OpenClaw plugin/tool guidance** and routed through `developer_instructions`, while user-editable workspace text and non-bundled SKILL.md content **remain user-reference scoped in turn input** per #83454. This is not a reversal of #83454: the user-editable surface that #83454 protected is left where #83454 put it; only the OpenClaw-owned bundled plugin-manifest surface moves, joining the workspace bootstrap files (#77372) that already live in `developer_instructions`.
- Related: builds on merged #77372 (lonexreb, workspace bootstrap → `developer_instructions`) as the lane-precedent for OpenClaw-owned stable instructions.
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- **Root cause**: The shared per-turn wrapper `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` conflated two fragments with different authority + lifetime needs:
  1. **Skills snapshot** (`params.skillsSnapshot?.prompt`) — a mixed-source catalog. OpenClaw-owned bundled skills (`openclaw-bundled`) are byte-stable, OpenClaw-controlled plugin manifest text, analogous to the workspace bootstrap files (`SOUL.md` / `IDENTITY.md` / `TOOLS.md` / `USER.md`) that already live in `developer_instructions` via #77372. The same field also carries SKILL.md frontmatter from workspace, project (`.agents/skills`), personal (`~/.agents/skills`), `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`, and plugin-generated sources whose content is user/install-controlled.
  2. **Workspace prompt context** (`workspaceBootstrapContext.promptContext`) — **user-editable** workspace files (e.g., `MEMORY.md`, custom project docs). Per #84662 §Security/rank caution, this must remain quoted/reference scoped.
  Bundling both into `turn/start.input[0].text` means the bytes ride the per-turn user-input lane and Codex native history persists one copy per turn. Issue #84662 carves these two halves apart explicitly; the wider scope from #84790 collapsed them back together and ran into the prompt-authority blocker raised in that review.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: No unit test pinned the authority boundary (workspace user-editable text must not appear in `developer_instructions`, and untrusted-source skill metadata must not appear in `developer_instructions`). This PR adds three: `thread-lifecycle.test.ts` "invariant: workspace user-editable context never appears in developer_instructions", `run-attempt.test.ts` "does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions", and `skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts` "trustedDeveloperPrompt includes bundled skills but excludes workspace/project/personal/managed/extra sources".
- **Contributing context**: cron and heartbeat triggers already populate `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` in production. The generated `@openai/codex` 0.132.0 `v2/TurnStartParams.ts` does not currently declare `collaborationMode` (the formal basis for #84790's [P1] protocol concern), but the bundled runtime binary parses and honors the field on cron / heartbeat triggers today — empirical evidence below shows it also honors it on the chat trigger.

## Security boundary

Trust-filter policy for the developer-instructions lane (`turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions`):

- **Allowed (elevated)**: skill entries whose `resolveSkillSource(entry.skill) === "openclaw-bundled"`. Only the OpenClaw-bundled catalog ships shipped/reviewed SKILL.md content and is treated as developer authority alongside the workspace bootstrap files (#77372). After `resolveWorkspaceSkillPromptState` in `src/agents/skills/workspace.ts` applies the shared `applySkillsPromptLimits` budget once over the combined `promptSkills`, the post-budget `skillsForPrompt` is partitioned by name into the trusted subset and rendered via `buildLaneSkillsPrompt` (XML shape from `formatSkillsForPrompt` / `formatSkillsCompact`) — identical byte shape to `prompt`, just restricted to the bundled entries.
- **Not elevated (kept in user/reference lane only)**: skill entries from `openclaw-workspace`, `agents-skills-project` (`.agents/skills`), `agents-skills-personal` (`~/.agents/skills`), `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`, plugin-generated, and any source not on the bundled allowlist. The trust check is deliberately conservative — when the source is unknown or absent it falls into the deny side. These entries are the untrusted half of the same post-budget partition and are rendered into `SkillSnapshot.untrustedReferencePrompt` via `buildLaneSkillsPrompt`; `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` emits them under an explicit `## OpenClaw User-Installed Skills (reference)` section nested in the existing `OpenClaw workspace context for this turn:` wrapper. Wrapper text states the section is reference metadata for tool discovery, not developer authority.
- **Legacy session migration**: `SkillSnapshot.schemaVersion` is stamped at every snapshot build (`SKILL_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 3`). The `agent-command.ts` reuse path runs `isSkillsSnapshotSchemaOutdated()`; snapshots whose `schemaVersion` is missing or below the current value (pre-PR v1 sessions; v2 sessions written by earlier commits on this branch before `remoteNote` was added) force-refresh through `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot` on the next turn so all four lane fields (`trustedDeveloperPrompt`, `untrustedReferencePrompt`, `remoteNote`, `schemaVersion`) are populated before the Codex call site reads them. Without this, the previous trust-filter HEAD would have silently dropped every skill (bundled or otherwise) from `developer_instructions` for existing sessions until a workspace bump or new session.
- **Remote-host execution guidance lane**: `SkillSnapshot.remoteNote` (populated by `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot` from `opts.eligibility.remote.note`) rides the non-authoritative per-turn user-input lane under the `OpenClaw Remote Skill Execution` reference section (described in the Summary). It is **not** elevated to `developer_instructions` — the same per-PR boundary that applies to user-installed skills also applies to runtime-supplied invocation hints. A unit test pins this (`run-attempt.test.ts` "routes skillsSnapshot.remoteNote through the per-turn reference lane, not developer_instructions").
- **Shared budget invariant**: `maxSkillsPromptChars` and `maxSkillsInPrompt` apply once over the combined catalog before the trusted/untrusted partition, so the model's combined view of skill content stays within the user's configured cap regardless of how the trust filter splits the catalog. A regression test pins this (`skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts` "applies the configured maxSkillsPromptChars budget across the combined trusted/untrusted lanes, not per-lane"); we verified it FAILs with the legacy per-lane budgeting and PASSes with the shared budget.
- **Wire-level invariants pinned by regression tests**:
  - `developer_instructions` carries only trust-filtered bundled skill descriptions/locations, even when an attacker controls workspace SKILL.md content (`run-attempt.test.ts`: "does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions").
  - Per-turn user input under the workspace-context wrapper surfaces non-bundled skill descriptions/locations under the reference section so native Codex stays able to discover them (`run-attempt.test.ts`: same case + "omits skills from developer_instructions when no trusted skills are present but keeps untrusted skills in the reference lane"; `run-attempt.test.ts`: "falls back to the user-input prompt only when no skills lanes are populated" for the no-op baseline).
  - The `SkillSnapshot` producer renders the bundled and non-bundled subsets into disjoint fragments (`skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts`: "trustedDeveloperPrompt includes bundled skills but excludes workspace/project/personal/managed/extra sources" + "untrustedReferencePrompt mirrors untrusted skill metadata for the reference lane" + "omits untrustedReferencePrompt when no non-bundled skills are present" + "stamps schemaVersion so legacy snapshots are force-refreshed").
  - The schema-version refresh predicate is correct across legacy / current / stale / future cases (`snapshot-hydration.test.ts`: 5 cases).
  - Provider-boundary precheck, `llm_input` observer, trajectory `context.compiled.systemPrompt`, and `result.systemPromptReport.skills.{promptChars,entries}` all reflect the trusted developer-lane bytes — not the full mixed catalog — so operators see the actual developer-lane size. The non-bundled bytes ride the per-turn user-input lane and are counted by Codex's input accounting separately.

  - Prompt-snapshot helper/check: `test/helpers/agents/happy-path-prompt-snapshots.ts`, `test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: (a) chat trigger renders trusted bundled skills as `developer_instructions`; (b) chat trigger without skills returns `null` `developer_instructions`; (c) cron composes cron + skills; (d) heartbeat composes heartbeat + overlay + skills; (e) cron without skills is byte-identical to current main; (f) workspace user-editable context never reaches `developer_instructions` (authority invariant); (g) untrusted skill metadata (workspace, project, personal, managed, extra) never reaches `developer_instructions`, even when `skillsSnapshot.prompt` carries it (prompt-authority boundary); (h) untrusted skill metadata remains visible to Codex through the per-turn user input under the reference wrapper (visibility-preserving fix for the post-trust-filter regression); (i) `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot` partitions skills into disjoint trusted/untrusted fragments and stamps `schemaVersion`; (j) legacy snapshots without `schemaVersion` force-refresh; (k) context-engine / prompt-snapshot surfaces report the workspace-only turn-input projection rather than the old runtime-context wrapper.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: each bug is structural (wrong lane plus wrong trust scope plus stale-snapshot reuse); the structural invariants pin all four halves (bundled skills present in `developer_instructions`, workspace absent from it, untrusted skill metadata absent from it, non-bundled skills present in the reference lane, legacy snapshots refreshed) without needing live-model evidence to rerun on every CI tick.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- **Per-turn token billing on the default `main` agent** (new turns after this PR ships): the **bundled** subset of the skills snapshot no longer re-bills in every `response.create.input`; the cumulative 10-turn `prompt_tokens` cost drops by ~24% on the ping-pong scenario in the Real-behavior proof. The **non-bundled** subset (workspace / project / personal / managed / extra / plugin-generated) continues to re-bill at the pre-#85646 rate because it is intentionally kept in the per-turn user-input lane for prompt-authority reasons (see Limitations). Workspace-context bytes continue to re-bill at the same rate as before (~+540/turn floor).
- **Per-turn user-input wrapper text** (new turns): renamed from `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:\n…` to `OpenClaw workspace context for this turn:\n…`. The block now contains (a) the existing `## OpenClaw Workspace Context` section, and (b) when any non-bundled skill is eligible, a new `## OpenClaw User-Installed Skills (reference)` section listing those skill names/descriptions/locations under a reference-only header.
- **Developer-instructions skills section** (new turns): only `openclaw-bundled` SKILL.md descriptions/locations appear here. SKILL.md from workspace, project, personal, `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`, and plugin-generated sources remains discoverable via the per-turn user-input reference section (visibility-preserving); it is never elevated to the developer-instructions lane.
- **Existing sessions opened before this PR ships**: the first turn after upgrade rebuilds the skills snapshot via `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot` so the new `trustedDeveloperPrompt` and `untrustedReferencePrompt` fields are populated; subsequent turns reuse the persisted snapshot in the normal way. Without this migration, the previous trust-filter HEAD would have silently dropped every skill from `developer_instructions` on those sessions until a workspace bump or new session.
- **Existing Codex native threads bound before this PR ships**: see "Limitations of this PR" below.
- **Snapshot fixtures**: three `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*.md` files regenerated to reflect the wrapper subline update.

## Limitations of this PR (deferred half explicitly disclosed)

This PR **prevents future bundled-skills bytes from being appended** to Codex native user history. It does **not** sanitize history for threads that were bound before the fix ships, and it does **not** eliminate per-turn user-history persistence of non-bundled SKILL.md content.

- **Non-bundled skills still ride the per-turn user-input lane**, by policy. Their SKILL.md descriptions/locations re-bill on every subsequent `response.create.input` and are persisted to native Codex user history — the same behavior as before #85646 for those entries. The alternative (elevating them to `developer_instructions`) was rejected as a prompt-authority regression by the round-2 ClawSweeper review; this PR keeps the visibility while explicitly disclosing the per-turn replay cost for non-bundled-heavy catalogs. Catalogs that are predominantly bundled (e.g. default OpenClaw installs) see the full ~24% cumulative token-savings benchmark below; catalogs with a non-trivial non-bundled subset see proportionally less savings on that subset.
- **Existing bound Codex native threads** that already contain `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-history items from prior turns will continue to replay those items on `response.create.input` until either (a) the thread cycles to a fresh native session, or (b) the deferred follow-up session-history sanitizer (issue #84662 §"Safe interim fix") lands as a stacked follow-up PR that strips the wrapper from `readCodexMirroredSessionHistoryMessages`.
- The snapshot-fixture check (`workspaceBootstrapContextFrom` field in `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*.md`) verifies the **current projection shape** for new turns; it is **not** a regression guard for the deferred sanitizer half — old mirrored-history user items are out of its scope.
- The 10-turn benchmark below was run at the prior trust-filter HEAD (`66ee52173e`, when the developer lane carried the full mixed-source catalog) with fresh user IDs. The current-head benchmark uplift on the trusted (bundled) developer lane is a **strict subset** of those gains — fewer bytes, identical wire shape, byte-stable across turns — so the cacheable / no-history-replay properties documented there continue to hold for the bundled subset. The non-bundled subset's per-turn replay cost matches the pre-#85646 baseline for that subset.

The deferred follow-up will credit `Co-authored-by: TurboTheTurtle` (PR #84790's sanitizer logic) and is independent of this PR; this PR remains complete and merge-ready on its own.

## Authority safety (why this is not the #84790 design)

| | PR #84790 (CLOSED 2026-05-22) | This PR |
|---|---|---|
| Content moved into `developer_instructions` | skills + workspace + MEMORY | **trust-filtered bundled skills only** |
| Skill source policy | full mixed-source catalog (bundled + workspace + project + personal + managed + extra + plugin) elevated | **`openclaw-bundled` only**; workspace / project / personal / managed / extra / plugin SKILL.md stays in user/reference lane under an explicit reference section header |
| Non-bundled skill visibility | implicit (they rode the same developer lane) | **explicit reference-lane wrapper**, visible to native Codex without developer authority |
| Workspace / MEMORY routing | through `developer_instructions` (with a disclaimer wrapper) | **unchanged from current `main`** — stays in turn-input reference lane established by #83454 |
| Session-state compatibility | session snapshots assumed to carry the new fields | **`SkillSnapshot.schemaVersion` migration** force-refreshes pre-PR snapshots so the new fields are populated on the next turn |
| Session-history sanitization | bundled in same PR | **deferred follow-up** (disclosed above) |
| Protocol-acceptance argument | schema inspection only | schema + production cron / heartbeat parity + wire-level Codex native rollout + cacheRead ratio uplift on the same scenario |
| Authority invariant test | n/a | three unit tests pin all three halves: workspace text never appears in `developer_instructions`, untrusted skill metadata never appears in `developer_instructions`, and untrusted skill metadata still appears in the per-turn user-input reference lane |

Reading order:

- Issue #84662 §"Security/rank caution": *"Stable instructions such as OpenClaw skills may be safe to move to developer/bootstrap instructions, but user-editable workspace context should remain quoted/reference scoped."* This PR follows that caveat literally and tightens it further: only **bundled** skill metadata (the OpenClaw-controlled half of the skills catalog) is treated as stable; user-installed SKILL.md content from workspace / project / personal / managed / extra / plugin sources stays in the reference lane.
- PR #84790 was closed by its author after the wider scope failed the prompt-authority finding raised in that review (wrapper text does not actually downgrade message authority).
- PR #83454 deliberately moved both halves *out* of `developer_instructions` so user-editable text would not gain developer authority. This PR complements that policy: workspace text and non-bundled SKILL.md content stay where #83454 put them; only the OpenClaw-owned bundled plugin-manifest skills snapshot — a stable, OpenClaw-controlled surface analogous to the bootstrap files in #77372 — moves to `developer_instructions`.
- PR #77372 already routes workspace-bootstrap files (`SOUL.md` / `IDENTITY.md` / `TOOLS.md` / `USER.md`) through `developer_instructions`; bundled skills join their natural peers under this PR.

## Real behavior proof

This PR carries two pieces of real-behavior evidence:

1. A **historical** 10-turn live OpenAI Codex benchmark captured at the prior trust-filter HEAD (`66ee52173e`, when the developer lane carried the full mixed-source skills catalog). The benchmark established the no-history-replay, byte-stable, cacheRead-uplift properties of the new lane.
2. A **current-head** wire-level proof artifact at `docs/research/runtime-context-surface-f1-trust-filter-current-head-proof.md` that exercises the production pipeline at the head SHA of this PR. It captures byte counts, sha256-12 hashes, and `contains`/`does-not-contain` assertions against synthetic marker strings (no live secrets, no private paths, no MEMORY content) for the trusted developer lane and the non-bundled reference lane, plus the legacy-snapshot refresh decision. Reproduce via `node --import tsx scripts/research/skills-trust-filter-proof.mts` or `./scripts/research/skills-trust-filter-proof.mts`.

The current-head trusted developer lane is a **strict reduction** of the bytes the historical benchmark sent (filter to bundled-only), so the historical benchmark's no-history-replay and byte-stability properties carry forward unchanged for the bundled subset. The non-bundled subset re-enters the per-turn user input lane under an explicit reference wrapper; its per-turn token cost matches the pre-#85646 baseline for that subset and is disclosed under Limitations.

**Historical 10-turn live benchmark (at `66ee52173e`)**

**Behavior addressed**: Codex app-server persists the OpenClaw skills snapshot as a per-turn user-history item in native Codex threads, causing the model's `response.create.input` to grow by ~2,194 tokens/turn on the default `main` agent (issue #84662 root cause).

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw checkout at the upstream/main HEAD `66ee52173e` was based on, rebuilt dist; live OpenAI Codex backend (`openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini`); two local OpenAI-compatible OpenClaw gateways on separate ports sharing the same eval state directory (one unpatched baseline, one with this PR's patch applied); a live 10-turn ping-pong benchmark as the proof surface. A separate live runtime instance on an unrelated port was observed before / during / after the experiment via `/healthz` to confirm no PID change in unrelated processes.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
- `node scripts/build-all.mjs`
- Start two OpenClaw gateways on separate ports against an eval `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, one built from current upstream/main (arm A — baseline) and one built from this PR's branch (used for arms A' and B), each launched via `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway` with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`.
- Drive a 10-turn `body_contains: "pong"` ping-pong scenario against each gateway via an OpenAI-compatible benchmark CLI, using a distinct user ID per arm (forces a fresh Codex native session per arm) for three arms:
  - Arm A — unpatched baseline (#84662 reproducible state), default `main` agent.
  - Arm A' — patched, default `main` agent.
  - Arm B — patched gateway, but a separately-configured agent with `tools.profile=minimal`, `systemPromptOverride="."`, `skills=[]` (i.e., a no-skills control).

**Evidence after fix (10-turn live benchmark × 3 arms, all 30/30 functional `body_contains: "pong"`)**:

| Arm | growth/turn | turn 1 / turn 10 prompt_tokens | cum 10t prompt_tokens | cacheRead/input (turn 9 steady) | uncached input tokens (turn 9) | skills.count / promptChars |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| A — unpatched baseline (#84662 reproducible state) | +2,194 | 21,286 / 41,032 | 311,590 | 93.27 % | 2,614 | 20 / 6,629 |
| A' — patched | **+535** | 21,166 / 25,981 | **235,735** | **96.08 %** | **998** | 20 / 6,557 |
| B — no-skills control (patched gateway, no-skills agent) | +540 | 19,552 / 24,412 | 219,820 | 95.98 % | 960 | 0 / 0 |

All three arms used **fresh** user IDs so each arm opened a new Codex native session — the patched-arm curve reflects the new-thread behavior, not the legacy-thread replay floor. Per the "Limitations" section above, legacy bound threads are out of scope for this proof.

- Per-turn growth on the default agent drops from +2,194 to +535 tokens/turn (**−75.6 %**); cumulative 10-turn save = **−75,855 tokens (−24.3 %)** vs the unpatched baseline.
- Arm B's no-skills floor (+540/turn) is within ±5 tokens/turn of arm A' — confirming the +535/turn residual on the patched arm is the workspace-context replay floor, **not** skills.
- Codex native rollout for the patched arm shows `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` populated with a byte-stable payload (md5 stable across all 10 turns at the historical HEAD; the current-head trusted-developer fragment is a strict reduction of those bytes so md5 stability properties carry forward).

**Latency**: trimmed mean **−448 ms (−8.8 %)** on the patched arm vs the baseline arm (5,102 → 4,654 ms after dropping hi/lo across n=10), both arms dominated by a single turn-1 cold-start outlier (A: 26,862 ms, A': 22,453 ms). No functional regression. We do **not** claim latency improvement at n=10; a 50-turn rerun would be the right place to make a stronger latency claim.

**Observed result after fix**: per-turn `prompt_tokens` growth on the default `main` agent drops from **+2,194 → +535 tokens/turn** (−75.6 %), within ±5 tokens/turn of the no-skills control (+540). Cumulative 10-turn `prompt_tokens` saves **−75,855 (−24.3 %)** vs the unpatched baseline. 30 / 30 functional `body_contains: "pong"` success across the 3 arms. `developer_instructions` is byte-stable across all 10 turns on the patched arm; workspace / MEMORY text never appears in `developer_instructions` (verified at the wire level and as a unit-test structural invariant). No functional regression observed.

**Current-head wire-level proof**

The current-head proof is reproducible from the repo and runs entirely against synthetic marker strings:

- **Source**: `scripts/research/skills-trust-filter-proof.mts`. Pinned shebang `#!/usr/bin/env -S node --import tsx`.
- **Artifact**: `docs/research/runtime-context-surface-f1-trust-filter-current-head-proof.md`. Regenerated whenever the script is rerun.
- **Coverage**: builds a real workspace fixture with one skill per source (`openclaw-bundled`, `openclaw-workspace`, `agents-skills-project`, `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`) and a MEMORY-marker workspace-context fragment, then captures (a) byte length + sha256-12 of `prompt`, `trustedDeveloperPrompt`, `untrustedReferencePrompt`, and the reconstructed Codex turn-input wrapper; (b) per-marker `MUST contain` / `MUST NOT contain` assertions for the developer lane and the reference lane; (c) `isSkillsSnapshotSchemaOutdated` decisions for both legacy (no `schemaVersion`) and current (schemaVersion=`SKILL_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION`) snapshots.
- **Result**: 23 wire assertions PASS, 0 FAIL. Legacy snapshot decision = `true` (force-refresh). Current snapshot decision = `false` (reuse).

**What was not tested**:

- A re-run of the 10-turn live benchmark against the current trust-filter HEAD. The change since `66ee52173e` is a strict reduction of bytes on the developer lane (filter to bundled-only) plus a non-authoritative addition to the reference lane; it does not change the wire shape, the byte-stability across turns, or the no-history-replay property the historical proof established for the trusted subset. The unit tests above and the current-head wire artifact pin the structural invariants the live benchmark would re-establish.
- Long-session 50 / 100-turn scaling under this change.
- Workspace-file mid-session edit cache-bust behaviour (workspace was static across the historical ~30 s experiment).
- Out-of-sample models (only `openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini` was exercised in the historical benchmark).
- Live Discord / Telegram production-channel turn (the OpenAI-compatible gateway was the historical proof surface, not a channel).
- **Legacy bound Codex native threads** that contain pre-fix `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-history items — by design (see "Limitations of this PR"). The deferred follow-up session-history sanitizer is what makes those threads stop replaying historical wrappers.
- Session-history sanitization itself, including the `readCodexMirroredSessionHistoryMessages` post-processing path — out of scope for this PR (deferred to stacked follow-up).
- `skills.promptChars` drifted by 72 chars between the two historical arms (6,629 vs 6,557) due to a bundled-plugin asset rebuild difference between the two checkouts, not the source patch; `skills.entries.count` is **20 / 20** in both arms and the same skill names appear in both arms' Codex native rollouts.
- A single turn-10 OpenAI cache-eviction anomaly on the historical patched arm reported `cached_input_tokens=10,624` (turn-1-like) and `uncached=15,357` against the byte-stable `developer_instructions` payload (md5 stable); turns 2–9 each showed ~96.1 % cache rate and the steady-state quoted above uses turn 9. The headline per-turn growth and cumulative `prompt_tokens` metrics are unaffected.
