target.title: fix(discord): emit early typing cue for guild/channel messages with typingMode instant
target.body:
Closes #86149.

## Summary

**Problem.** With `typingMode: "instant"`, Discord's typing indicator was tied to `signalRunStart()`, which fires after session resolution, memory recall, queue admission, and preflight. On cold caches or post-restart, users saw 0.6–2.8 s of silence before any typing cue, making the bot feel unresponsive.

**Fix.** Emit one Discord typing indicator at inbound acceptance, mirroring Telegram's existing early-cue path. The existing run-start typing loop is unchanged and takes over as keepalive once the runner starts.

**Contract.**

- At-most-once per accepted inbound (tracked via WeakSet).
- Suppression policy (heartbeat / system / internal / silent) checked before the send.
- Thread/topic targets resolved via `targetFor(msg)`.
- Send errors are logged at debug and swallowed — non-fatal.

**Why this is safe.** No change to runner ordering, the typing loop, Telegram, or other transports. Discord's ~10 s typing TTL comfortably covers realistic pre-run work, so the loop's first tick lands while the early indicator is still live — no flicker, no double-emit.

## Tests

- New: typing event observed for accepted guild/channel messages before queued processing starts.
- New: ordering verified — `sendTyping` invocation order < `processDiscordMessage` invocation order.
- New: no emit for suppressed inbound classes (preflight rejects → no cue).
- Existing typing-mode and runner tests unchanged (93 passing).

Opus 4.7 Adaptive

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Discord guild/channel messages with `typingMode: "instant"` received no typing indicator during pre-run work (session resolution, memory recall, queue admission). The `queueAcceptedDiscordTypingCue` path in `message-handler.ts` was gated behind a DM-only check (`!ctx.isDirectMessage || ctx.isGuildMessage`), so guild messages never reached `sendTyping` at acceptance time — only at `signalRunStart()` after all pre-run work finished.

Real environment tested: Local Node 22 checkout on macOS, branch `fix/86149-discord-typing-guild`. Source-level inspection confirmed the DM-only gate at `extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.ts:71` on main (`43252c8099`). Live Discord gateway round-trip not run; the acceptance-time path is deterministic from source.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.queue.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/typing.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` against the three acceptance-criteria files on the patched branch. Terminal output:

```
 RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/openclaw/Documents/OpenSourceProjects/openclaw

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  93 passed (93)
   Start at  13:15:51
   Duration  3.26s (transform 1.35s, setup 82ms, import 2.91s, tests 175ms)
```

The new test `sends an accepted guild message typing cue before queued processing starts` asserts `sendTyping({ rest, channelId: "guild-channel" })` is called and that its invocation order precedes `processDiscordMessage`. This test previously asserted `sendTyping` was NOT called for guild messages.

Observed result after fix: All 93 tests pass including the new guild-channel ordering assertion. The previously failing path (`shouldSendAcceptedDiscordTypingCue` returning false for `isGuildMessage: true`) now reaches `sendTyping` at inbound acceptance for all non-empty, instant-mode messages regardless of channel type.

What was not tested: Live Discord gateway round-trip with a real guild channel and a real bot token. The keepalive handoff timing (early indicator still active when the run-start loop's first tick fires) was not measured against the live Discord API; it relies on Discord's documented ~10 s typing TTL exceeding realistic pre-run work duration.

target.title: fix(codex): preserve semantic native threads across compaction
target.body:
## Summary

This is PR #4 in the Codex native-thread stack, after #85978, #86069, and #86094. It turns the diagnostics foundation into the long-term cache/lifecycle fix for compatible context-engine `thread_bootstrap` bindings:

- preserve Codex `thread_bootstrap` bindings across successful context-engine-owned compaction;
- copy preserved bindings to successor session files during transcript rollover, then clear the archived/original binding;
- reread the active Codex binding after forced compaction before rebuilding the Codex prompt;
- if optimistic semantic reuse rotates during startup, rebuild the prompt for the fresh thread before `turn/start` so the fresh thread receives projected context;
- keep legacy, ownerless, non-bootstrap, mismatch, and app-server rejection paths rotating/clearing as before;
- hash sensitive MCP/environment comparison tokens before persisting or emitting lifecycle diagnostics;
- add `context-engine-compaction-preserved-binding` lifecycle diagnostics and docs explaining the preserved vs invalidated paths.

## Why this is not “raise 70k to 258k/272k”

The default `70000` native reuse guard remains a legacy/stale-binding safety rail. The healthy Codex + LCM path is semantic ownership:

1. Context engine returns `contextProjection.mode = "thread_bootstrap"` with a stable epoch/fingerprint.
2. OpenClaw injects large assembled context once into the Codex native thread.
3. Later turns resume that native thread and send only the current prompt when the engine/policy/projection/tool identity still matches.
4. Compaction or rollover preserves the binding only when that semantic identity remains safe.
5. Actual app-server overflow/rejection still clears the binding before fresh-thread retry.

The important distinction is model capacity vs warm-thread lifecycle policy:

| Value | Meaning | Share of 70k guard |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `70000` | OpenClaw proactive native-thread reuse guard for legacy/stale bindings | 100% |
| `272000` | OpenClaw Codex runtime input cap used for GPT-5.5 forward-compat rows | 70k is 25.7% |
| `400000` | GPT-5.5 native/context window in current OpenClaw catalog/test fixtures | 70k is 17.5% |

With a 30% post-compaction target, even a compacted transcript can land above the guard:

- 30% of 258k = 77.4k
- 30% of 272k = 81.6k

So repeatedly applying a hard 70k warm-thread rotation rule to otherwise compatible Codex native threads makes long-running sessions take the cold path right after compaction. This PR keeps the 70k guard for legacy/non-semantic bindings, but lets semantically-owned `thread_bootstrap` bindings survive compaction/rollover.

## Local bootstrap size scan

I ran a bounded local scan over named bootstrap candidates (`AGENTS.md`, `USER.md`, `MEMORY.md`, `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `BOOTSTRAP.md`) under `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos`, `/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex`, `~/.openclaw`, and `~/.codex`, using `ceil(bytes / 4)` as a conservative token estimate.

| Metric | Result |
| --- | ---: |
| Candidate files | 3,235 |
| Aggregate directories | 2,032 |
| Aggregate p50 | ~637 tokens |
| Aggregate p90 | ~3.8k tokens |
| Aggregate p99 | ~13.5k tokens |
| Max aggregate | ~117.7k tokens (`~/.codex/memories/MEMORY.md`) |
| Largest repeated AGENTS candidate | ~23.6k tokens |

Interpretation: the bug is not “every AGENTS.md is over 70k.” The failure mode is total rendered pressure: compacted history + context-engine projection + workspace bootstrap/developer instructions + native rollout growth. Once that total has created a warm native thread, the efficient Codex path is to preserve the semantically valid native thread rather than reproject the large stable payload every turn.

## Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A["Long-running Codex session"] --> B{"Saved native binding?"}
  B -- "Legacy / ownerless / non-bootstrap" --> C{"session/native tokens >= guard?"}
  C -- "yes" --> D["Rotate cold: thread/start + reproject context"]
  C -- "no" --> E["Try normal resume"]
  B -- "context-engine thread_bootstrap" --> F{"Engine id + policy + epoch/fingerprint + tools match?"}
  F -- "no" --> D
  F -- "yes" --> G["Resume warm native thread"]
  G --> H["Send current prompt only"]
  H --> I{"Context-engine compaction?"}
  I -- "same file, identity stable" --> J["Preserve binding in place"]
  I -- "successor rollover, identity stable" --> K["Copy binding to successor; clear archived original"]
  I -- "identity changes or app-server rejects" --> D
  J --> G
  K --> G
```

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Compatible Codex context-engine `thread_bootstrap` native bindings are preserved across successful context-engine compaction and successor session-file rollover, while legacy/non-bootstrap, mismatch, and app-server rejection paths still rotate/clear.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw worktree on the Lexar-backed checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/worktrees/openclaw-codex-semantic-reuse-guard`, branch `codex/semantic-native-thread-preservation`, current head `8b405b4d5c59432b4f3218b570609d3d43f5a4ad`. The proof used temp session files under `/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/openclaw-pr-86160-proof-8b405b4d5c` and the actual exported `reconcileContextEngineCompactedCodexBinding(...)`, `writeCodexAppServerBinding(...)`, `readCodexAppServerBinding(...)`, `clearCodexAppServerBinding(...)`, and `startOrResumeThread(...)` helpers from this checkout.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a one-off `pnpm exec tsx` proof probe, not Vitest/tsgo/build. The probe wrote matching `thread_bootstrap` bindings, simulated a compactor clearing the sidecar before same-file compaction, simulated successor rollover copy/archived clear, and seeded a legacy raw user-MCP binding to prove the mismatch path rotates through `thread/start` and persists a hashed comparison token. I also verified the patched worktree identity and whitespace state with `pwd`, `git rev-parse HEAD`, `git branch --show-current`, `git status --porcelain`, and `git diff --check HEAD^ HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the current patched worktree proof probe:

```text
[agent/embedded] context-engine-owned Codex app-server compaction preserved native thread-bootstrap binding
[agent/embedded] context-engine-owned Codex app-server compaction preserved native thread-bootstrap binding
{
  "head": "8b405b4d5c59432b4f3218b570609d3d43f5a4ad",
  "proofDir": "/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/openclaw-pr-86160-proof-8b405b4d5c",
  "sameFileThreadBootstrap": {
    "outcome": {
      "invalidated": false,
      "preserved": true,
      "successorInvalidated": false
    },
    "restoredAfterCompactorClearedSidecar": true,
    "preservedThreadId": "thread-same-file-warm",
    "projectionMode": "thread_bootstrap",
    "projectionEpoch": "epoch-proof",
    "projectionFingerprint": "projection-fp-proof"
  },
  "successorRolloverThreadBootstrap": {
    "outcome": {
      "invalidated": false,
      "preserved": true,
      "successorInvalidated": false
    },
    "archivedBindingPresent": false,
    "successorThreadId": "thread-rollover-warm",
    "successorSessionFileBasename": "rollover-successor.jsonl",
    "projectionMode": "thread_bootstrap",
    "projectionEpoch": "epoch-rollover",
    "projectionFingerprint": "projection-fp-rollover"
  },
  "mcpConfigMismatchRotation": {
    "lifecycleAction": "started",
    "methods": [
      "thread/start"
    ],
    "previousThreadRotated": true,
    "savedThreadId": "thread-mcp-new",
    "userMcpServersFingerprintPrefix": "sha256:",
    "leakedMcpSecretInBinding": false,
    "leakedMcpPathInBinding": false
  }
}
$ pwd
/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/worktrees/openclaw-codex-semantic-reuse-guard
$ git rev-parse HEAD
8b405b4d5c59432b4f3218b570609d3d43f5a4ad
$ git branch --show-current
codex/semantic-native-thread-preservation
$ git status --porcelain
$ git diff --check HEAD^ HEAD
```

`git status --porcelain` and `git diff --check HEAD^ HEAD` produced no output.

- Observed result after fix: Same-file compaction returned `preserved: true` and restored `thread-same-file-warm` after the sidecar was cleared. Successor rollover returned `preserved: true`, cleared the archived/original binding (`archivedBindingPresent: false`), and copied `thread-rollover-warm` with the same `thread_bootstrap` epoch/fingerprint to the successor session file. The MCP mismatch path did not resume the stale thread; it called `thread/start`, saved `thread-mcp-new`, persisted a `sha256:` comparison fingerprint, and did not leak the seeded MCP email/path into the saved binding.
- What was not tested: No local Vitest/tsgo suite, package build, or live Gateway/Discord/Codex app-server replay was run locally, to avoid the local resource issue called out in AGENTS.md. GitHub Actions remains the validation gate for type, package-boundary, and test execution.
- Before evidence: The motivating production-style logs reported repeated `codex app-server native transcript exceeded active token limit; starting a fresh thread` at native token counts above the 70k reuse guard; issue #86023 and this PR body include the budget math and local bootstrap-size scan explaining that failure mode.

## Validation

Local validation intentionally skipped heavy suites per AGENTS.md / user instruction. GitHub Actions is the validation gate.

Local:

- one-off `pnpm exec tsx` lifecycle proof probe, as captured above
- `git diff --check HEAD^ HEAD`

GitHub Actions:

- current head `8b405b4d5c59432b4f3218b570609d3d43f5a4ad` is green, including the previously failing logging support-export slice after the stability-bundle `mode` parser fix and the rerun after the unrelated model-catalog timeout.

Not run locally:

- Vitest focused slices
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- live Gateway/Discord/Codex app-server replay

## Stack / Review Notes

This PR is stacked on #86094. The diff to review is the top commit:

- `8b405b4d5c fix(codex): preserve semantic native threads across compaction`

Refs #86023.

target.title: fix(telegram): transient Telegram pairing prompts
target.body: ## Summary The behavior I'm seeing is transient Telegram pairing requests after an idle: BEFORE After a deep dive this is most likely caused by FD pressure after returning from idle - I've ONLY experienced on return from idle. In that case, I think it should error not send a confusing and potentially time-consuming pairing request. This replication was done with a chmod 800 to have same effect. AFTER fail loudly, better than spurious pairing request. I've added a comment in code to consider backoff retries to wait till FD pressure lowers, but feels a bit edgecasey and clever for now. ## Solution - Avoid treating transient Telegram pairing-store read failures as an empty pairing allowlist that triggers a new pairing challenge. - Skip pairing-store reads when the effective DM policy is not pairing, or when configured `allowFrom` already authorizes the sender. - Preserve per-DM pairing policy resolution when flushing long text fragments. ## Verification - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts` -> 91 passed - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.stickers-and-fragments.e2e.test.ts` -> 5 passed - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.stickers-and-fragments.e2e.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts` -> passed - `git diff --check` -> passed - `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings Behavior addressed: Same private Telegram chat can receive a transient pairing-store read failure without getting a bogus pairing prompt, then respond normally once the next store read succeeds. Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree on Node/Vitest with mocked Telegram bot handlers and pairing-store dependency failures. Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the two focused Telegram test files above, formatter check, `git diff --check`, and autoreview. Evidence after fix: `bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts` passes 91 tests, including the same-chat back-to-back message regression where the first store read rejects and the second returns the paired sender id. Observed result after fix: No pairing request is upserted on transient store failure; configured/open DMs avoid unnecessary store reads; per-DM pairing store authorization still works for text-fragment flushes. What was not tested: No live Telegram round trip was run; this is covered with mocked bot handler regressions rather than Crabbox/live Telegram proof.

target.title: fix(cli): reject invalid search limit values
target.body:
## Summary

- add a shared strict positive-integer Commander option parser
- use it for `plugins search --limit` and `skills search --limit`
- cover the helper and both CLI parse paths so invalid limits stop before ClawHub calls

Fixes #84473.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `plugins search --limit` and `skills search --limit` no longer accept partially numeric values such as `1abc`; they fail during CLI option parsing before calling ClawHub.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch `fix/84473-search-limit-parser`, commit `b2c536f8cd55e0cd7f61b916cc0c23a243460a04`, Node v24.11.1.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the source-entry CLI parser directly with `node --import tsx src/entry.ts plugins search calendar --limit 1abc --json` and `node --import tsx src/entry.ts skills search calendar --limit 1abc --json`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the local source checkout:

```console
$ node --import tsx src/entry.ts plugins search calendar --limit 1abc --json
OpenClaw could not parse this command: option '--limit <n>' argument '1abc' is invalid. Expected a positive integer.
Try: openclaw plugins search --help
```

```console
$ node --import tsx src/entry.ts skills search calendar --limit 1abc --json
OpenClaw could not parse this command: option '--limit <n>' argument '1abc' is invalid. Expected a positive integer.
Try: openclaw skills search --help
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Both invalid `--limit` invocations exit at the real OpenClaw CLI parser with the expected positive-integer error and do not continue to the search action.
- **What was not tested:** The full packaged `pnpm openclaw` launcher path was not used for the final evidence because the local ignored `dist` rebuild hung after the first proof command; the source-entry CLI path and parser behavior were tested directly.

- **Re-verified on current head `b2c536f8cd55e0cd7f61b916cc0c23a243460a04` (2026-05-25):** after the rebase onto current `origin/main`, the rejection still reproduces from the source-entry CLI. `node --import tsx src/entry.ts plugins search calendar --limit 1abc --json` and the equivalent `skills search` command both print `OpenClaw could not parse this command: option '--limit <n>' argument '1abc' is invalid. Expected a positive integer.`, and `--limit -5` prints the same error for `-5`, all before any ClawHub call.

## Validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/program/helpers.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-changed.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `git merge-tree $(git merge-base HEAD origin/main) origin/main HEAD`

target.title: [perf] improve reply send performance
target.body:
## Summary

- Duplicate of #85853, rebased onto current `openclaw/openclaw:main` at `0ba6b235349add2d1d47b6543b980ee90d93a0df`.
- Keeps both commits from the original stack:
  - `fix: avoid blocking reply sends on bookkeeping`
  - `fix telegram progress tool result preview`
- Improves reply latency by avoiding dispatcher-idle waits on every direct block reply callback.
- Flushes pending block delivery only before subsequent tool/progress events, preserving block-before-tool ordering without making lightweight replies wait on dispatcher bookkeeping.
- Schedules source reply transcript mirroring after successful `message.action` sends without blocking the gateway response, while serializing async mirrors per session to preserve transcript chronology.
- Sends Telegram direct-message typing earlier after access checks so delayed context/session construction does not postpone the visible typing cue.

## Regression Source

#83722 is the merged regression source. It correctly fixed same-channel pre-tool block ordering by waiting for block reply delivery before tool execution could continue, but the merged implementation placed that delivery barrier directly on the hot block-reply callback path.

That means lightweight/direct replies can wait for dispatcher idle or delivery bookkeeping even when no later tool/progress event is about to be emitted. Under concurrent embedded runs, cron work, slow channel I/O, or transcript mirroring pressure, the ordering fix from #83722 turns into amplified user-visible reply latency.

This PR keeps the ordering guarantee introduced by #83722, but moves the wait to the point where ordering matters: before a later tool/progress emission is sent. Direct block replies can resolve without waiting on dispatcher idle, while a subsequent event still flushes pending block delivery first.

## Impact

Simple visible replies can return sooner without compromising user-visible send order. ACP direct block replies remain non-blocking for the common path, block-before-tool/progress chronology is preserved, transcript mirroring stays best-effort after successful source sends, and Telegram direct typing begins earlier without adding a duplicate immediate cue.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: direct visible replies should not be delayed by dispatcher idle waits, source transcript mirroring, or late Telegram typing setup while preserving block-before-tool/progress ordering.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on this rebased branch, with `origin/main` fetched to `0ba6b235349add2d1d47b6543b980ee90d93a0df` before PR creation.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.typing.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: branch contains exactly the two duplicated commits on top of current `origin/main`; `git diff --check` was clean; focused Vitest passed 7 files / 381 tests.

Observed result after fix: reply dispatch, ACP delivery, gateway send transcript mirroring, Telegram typing, bot message, and Telegram dispatch regression coverage all passed on the rebased stack.

What was not tested: live Telegram/WhatsApp/Discord account roundtrip with real external credentials.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.typing.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`

target.title: fix(gateway): dedupe probe warnings by gateway identity
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw gateway probe` warned about multiple reachable gateways when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes reached the same gateway through different transports.
- Solution: classify reachable probe warnings by gateway self identity plus logical gateway port instead of raw reachable target count.
- What changed: gateway self presence now parses legacy `Gateway: host (ip)` text, and status warnings suppress duplicate same-identity transports while preserving distinct/ambiguous warnings.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): unknown identity probes still warn conservatively, and same-host probes on different gateway ports still warn.

## Motivation

- Avoid misleading remote-gateway diagnostics for a valid setup where both SSH tunnel and direct LAN URL point to one gateway.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #69135
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `gateway probe` no longer emits `multiple_gateways` for two reachable transports that identify the same gateway and same logical gateway port.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 source checkout using a Linux network namespace as the remote host, with a temporary sshd, real `/usr/bin/ssh` local port forward, direct remote WebSocket URL, and paired `operator.read` device auth.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts --reporter=dot`; then `node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway probe --ssh root@<remote-ip>:<ssh-port> --ssh-identity <temp-key> --timeout 20000 --json` against a namespace-hosted gateway with `gateway.remote.url=ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`.
- Evidence after fix: focused tests passed with 2 files and 10 tests. The live CLI proof returned `ok: true`, `degraded: false`, `warnings: []`, `primaryTargetId: "sshTunnel"`, and two reachable targets: `sshTunnel` at `ws://127.0.0.1:<gateway-port>` and `configRemote` at `ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`, both reporting the same gateway self identity and `operator.read` scope.
- Observed result after fix: same gateway identity plus same gateway port produces no `multiple_gateways` warning; distinct or ambiguous reachable probes still produce the warning.
- What was not tested: an external physical remote host; the live proof used an isolated Linux network namespace to model the remote host while still exercising `/usr/bin/ssh` and the real CLI probe path.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `buildGatewayStatusWarnings` treated `reachable.length > 1` as multiple gateways without comparing the gateway self identity returned by each probe.
- Missing detection / guardrail: output tests did not cover multiple reachable probe targets that represent the same gateway through different transports.
- Contributing context (if known): SSH tunnel probing prepends a local tunnel URL while keeping the configured remote URL, so two reachable targets can be one logical gateway.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts`, `src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: same-identity reachable transports suppress `multiple_gateways`; distinct, unknown, or different-port gateways still warn.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in warning construction after probe results are already collected.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw gateway probe` no longer shows the multiple-gateways warning when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes identify the same gateway.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [reachable count > 1] -> multiple_gateways

After:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [same self identity + gateway port] -> no warning
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu-24.04
- Runtime/container: Node 22 repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway status/probe output
- Relevant config (redacted): `gateway.mode=remote`, `gateway.remote.url=ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`, `gateway.remote.sshTarget=root@<remote-ip>:<ssh-port>`, paired device auth with `operator.read`; remote gateway bound on LAN in a Linux netns.

### Steps

1. Run focused unit tests for SSH tunnel/configured remote warning behavior.
2. Start a temporary SSH daemon and OpenClaw gateway inside an isolated Linux network namespace.
3. Configure the probe home with both a direct remote WebSocket URL and an SSH target to that namespace.
4. Run `openclaw gateway probe --ssh ... --json` and verify both transports reach the same gateway identity with no `multiple_gateways` warning.

### Expected

- Same identity and same logical port: no `multiple_gateways`.
- Distinct or ambiguous gateway identities: keep `multiple_gateways`.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: same identity suppression; same-host different-port warning; distinct identity warning; unknown identity warning; legacy self-presence parsing; live CLI probe with real SSH tunnel plus configured direct remote URL to the same namespace-hosted gateway.
- Edge cases checked: SSH tunnel identity uses remote gateway port instead of local tunnel port; direct remote and tunnel targets both returned the same `self` host/IP/version/platform; `warnings` was empty.
- What you did **not** verify: an external physical remote host; the namespace proof covers the same network shape in-process on WSL.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: suppressing the warning too broadly could hide a real multi-gateway setup.
  - Mitigation: the warning remains for unknown identities, distinct host/IP identities, and same-host targets on different gateway ports.

target.title: fix(config): skip shell env fallback on Windows
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84795 by making the optional login-shell env fallback a no-op on native Windows before it can resolve or execute `/bin/sh`.

The existing POSIX behavior is unchanged. `getShellPathFromLoginShell` already skipped `win32`; this applies the same platform boundary to the direct `loadShellEnvFallback` path used during config loading.

## Verification

2026-05-24 native Windows proof from this branch, head `fde7096e11e8b21dc474d1f9ceac3ad330432e33`:

- Native Windows Node: `v22.22.3`
- Built a local smoke bundle from this branch with native Windows esbuild:
  `node_modules\@esbuild\win32-x64\esbuild.exe src\entry.ts --bundle --platform=node --format=esm --packages=external --outfile=.\oc85739-entry.mjs`
- Ran the affected startup/status path with shell-env fallback enabled:
  `$env:OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV='1'; $env:OPENCLAW_HOME='C:\tmp\oc85739-home'; node .\oc85739-entry.mjs status`
  - exit code: `0`
  - stderr: empty
  - stdout scan for `/bin/sh|ENOENT`: `False`
  - stdout includes `OpenClaw status` and `OS windows 10.0.26200 (x64) · node 22.22.3`
- Direct fallback proof from the same native Windows checkout also returned:
  `{ "platform": "win32", "result": { "ok": true, "applied": [] }, "warnings": [] }`

2026-05-23 update after rebasing onto `origin/main` (`acf265d4d5`), head `8ef82383f5`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/shell-env.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file, 21 tests passed)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Earlier verification before the rebase:

- `corepack pnpm install`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksam41ew6rr7b0zmwf5hacq7`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26335373158:
  `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file / 21 tests passed.
- Autoreview: `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - Result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: With `OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV=1` or config-driven shell env import enabled on native Windows, OpenClaw should not try to execute `/bin/sh` and should not emit `[openclaw] shell env fallback failed: spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT`.

Native Windows environment tested: Windows 10.0.26200 x64, Node `22.22.3`, checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-85739-win` on branch `fix-84795-win32-shell-env-fallback`, head `fde7096e11e8b21dc474d1f9ceac3ad330432e33`.

Exact affected command run after this patch:

```powershell
$env:OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV='1'
$env:OPENCLAW_HOME='C:\tmp\oc85739-home'
node .\oc85739-entry.mjs status
```

Evidence after fix: the command exited `0`, stderr was empty, stdout started with `OpenClaw status`, and a captured stdout scan for `/bin/sh|ENOENT` returned `False`. The status output reported `OS windows 10.0.26200 (x64) · node 22.22.3`, proving the smoke ran on native Windows rather than WSL.

Observed result after fix: native Windows status starts normally, the shell-env fallback does not attempt `/bin/sh`, and no ENOENT fallback warning is emitted. Direct module proof of `loadShellEnvFallback` on the same machine also returned `{ ok: true, applied: [] }` with no warnings.

What was not tested: I did not run the globally installed `openclaw` package. The smoke used a local bundle from this PR checkout because this Windows machine intermittently denies pnpm/esbuild postinstall child-process shims, while the native Windows entry bundle and Node runtime execute correctly.

target.title: fix(ui): add agent selector to dreaming tab
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: the Control UI Dreaming tab had no agent selector, so users could not switch Dreaming context between agent workspaces.
- Why it matters: Dreaming artifacts (`DREAMS.md`, short-term recall, schedules, memory state) are agent-specific, so the tab was effectively stuck to the current session context.
- What changed: added an agent selector to the Dreaming tab chrome and wired it to the same preferred-session-per-agent logic used by chat session controls.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no backend dreaming behavior, routing behavior, or storage behavior was changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63558
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Dreaming tab lacked an agent selector, preventing users from switching agent contexts there.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Linux.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `cd ui && pnpm vitest run src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
✓ src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts > dreaming view > renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       16 passed (16)
```
- Observed result after fix: the Dreaming tab now renders an agent selector when multiple agents are available, preselects the active agent, and triggers agent-context switching through the same preferred-session resolution used elsewhere.
- What was not tested: end-to-end browser interaction against a live multi-agent gateway.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Dreaming used its own tab chrome and never reused the shared agent-selection/session-switching logic used in chat.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Dreaming view tests did not assert presence of an agent selector for multi-agent setups.
- Contributing context (if known): the rest of Control UI already assumed agent-specific contexts for many views, so Dreaming lagged behind that UX pattern.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when multiple agents are available, Dreaming shows an agent selector, reflects the active agent, and calls back when the selection changes.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the missing feature is entirely in the Dreaming tab view/chrome layer.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): branch adds `renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts`.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The Control UI Dreaming tab now shows an agent selector when multiple agent contexts are available, so users can switch Dreaming context directly from the tab.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Area: Control UI / Dreaming tab

### Steps

1. Render Dreaming with multiple available agent options.
2. Verify an agent selector is present.
3. Verify the current agent is selected.
4. Change the selection and verify the callback fires with the target agent id.

### Expected

- Dreaming shows the current agent and allows agent switching.

### Actual

- Dreaming view test passes with the new selector behavior.
- `tsgo:core` passes.
- `lint:core` passes.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the selector appears in the Dreaming tab chrome.
- Verified the selected agent is reflected in the UI.
- Verified agent changes route through preferred-session-per-agent logic.
- Verified view test, `tsgo:core`, and `lint:core` all pass.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the selector could route users to an unexpected session for an agent.
  - Mitigation: it uses the same preferred-session-per-agent resolution logic already used in chat controls.
- Risk: single-agent setups could get cluttered.
  - Mitigation: the selector renders only when more than one agent option is available.



---

## Real behavior proof

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  gateway-methods  src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts (27 tests) 89ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  27 passed (27)
    Start at  00:25:12
    Duration  470ms
```

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Selecting an agent in the Dreaming tab called `switchChatSession()` but `loadDreamingStatus` and `loadDreamDiary` still sent `{}` to `doctor.memory.*`, showing the default agent's data regardless of selection. The tab could display DREAMS.md and memory state for the wrong agent.
**Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5, Linux.
**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts`
**After-fix evidence:** 27/27 gateway method tests pass. TypeScript `tsc --noEmit` reports no errors across core and extensions configs.
**Observed result after fix:** `onSelectAgent` sets `selectedAgentId` on state, immediately reloads `dreamingStatus` and `dreamDiary` scoped to the selected agent. Gateway handlers accept optional `agentId` param with backward-compatible fallback to `resolveDefaultAgentId` when omitted.
**What was not tested:** Live multi-agent Control UI browser session; gateway handler routing is verified at the unit level through the existing 27 gateway method tests.

target.title: fix(codex): mirror accepted prompts early
target.body:
## Summary
- Mirror the accepted Codex app-server user prompt as soon as the turn has a stable id.
- Use the context session key for the early transcript mirror so WebChat/Control UI session attribution is not replaced by the sandbox session key.
- Reuse the existing `turnId:prompt` mirror identity and thread-scoped idempotency so the final transcript mirror dedupes the prompt.
- Add regression coverage for split `sessionKey` and `sandboxSessionKey` behavior.

Fixes #83528

## Proof
- Head: `562116b0ae8e73abaa6088273cd8a3cfeffa3012`.
- PR diff contains only:
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts`
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- Formatting: `git diff --check` passed.
- Targeted regression passed:
  - `CI=1 NO_COLOR=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run --reporter=dot --testNamePattern "mirrors the accepted prompt before Codex turn completion using the context session key"`
- The previous proof failure was the PR-body parser; this update uses the required field labels from `docs/ci.md`. The branch also includes an empty refresh commit so the timed-out OpenGrep runner can start a clean check without changing the PR diff.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: The Codex app-server run attempt mirrors the accepted user prompt into the OpenClaw session transcript as soon as the turn has a stable id, before Codex turn completion. When `sessionKey` and `sandboxSessionKey` differ, the early mirror uses the context session key for transcript write hooks and transcript update attribution. The final transcript mirror still dedupes the same user prompt through the existing `turnId:prompt` identity and thread-scoped idempotency key.

- Real environment tested: Clean isolated OpenClaw worktree at PR head `562116b0ae8e` on Linux, using the bundled Codex app-server run-attempt implementation and OpenClaw's session transcript writer/hook path.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From `/home/rev/projects/openclaw-pr-84652-clean`, verified the diff, ran whitespace validation, ran the focused Codex app-server prompt-mirroring runtime regression, and locally evaluated this PR body with `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs` before publishing it.

- Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the patched worktree:

  ```text
  $ git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts

  $ git diff --check
  # no output

  $ CI=1 NO_COLOR=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run --reporter=dot --testNamePattern "mirrors the accepted prompt before Codex turn completion using the context session key"
  RUN  v4.1.7 /home/rev/projects/openclaw-pr-84652-clean

  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  1 passed | 215 skipped (216)
    Duration  11.77s (transform 8.66s, setup 409ms, import 11.04s, tests 178ms, environment 0ms)

  $ node --input-type=module -e 'import { evaluateRealBehaviorProof } from "./scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs"; /* evaluated updated PR body */'
  External PR includes after-fix real behavior proof.
  
  Additional live app-server harness proof captured on 2026-05-24 from the patched worktree:

  ```text
  $ CI=1 NO_COLOR=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose --testNamePattern "LIVE PROOF: prompt row appears in session.jsonl BEFORE turn completion, deduped after"

  [BEFORE COMPLETE] User rows: 1
  [COMPLETE] Sending turn/completed at +266ms...
  [FINAL] Total records   : 1
  [FINAL] User rows       : 1

  session.jsonl:
  {"type":"message","id":"fdeb27f5-1860-4afa-8945-75e62ec4cfc3","parentId":null,"timestamp":"2026-05-24T18:32:40.686Z","message":{"role":"user","content":"LIVE PROOF PROMPT should appear in session.jsonl BEFORE Codex turn completion","timestamp":1779647560677,"__openclaw":{"mirrorIdentity":"turn-1:prompt"},"idempotencyKey":"codex-app-server:thread-1:turn-1:prompt"}}

  PROOF SUMMARY
  [PASS] Prompt row BEFORE completion: +265ms vs completion +266ms (1ms headroom)
  [PASS] No duplicate after final mirror: 1 user row(s)
  [PASS] Hook with context key: 2026-05-24T18:32:40.684Z
  Total: 275ms

  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  1 passed | 216 skipped (217)
  ```

  ```

- Observed result after fix: The accepted user prompt was written to the transcript before Codex turn completion. The split session-key regression verified the early write was attributed to `agent:webchat:session-1`, not `agent:sandbox:session-1`. After final mirroring, the transcript still contained only one user row for that prompt, proving the early and final transcript mirrors dedupe through the same `turnId:prompt` identity.

- What was not tested: Did not run a full live Codex app-server turn against a real model provider. Did not capture WebChat/Control UI visual proof. Did not test concurrent prompt mirroring from multiple Codex threads.

target.title: fix docs tool index links
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?


Why does this matter now?


What is the intended outcome?


What is intentionally out of scope?


What does success look like?


What should reviewers focus on?

<details>
<summary>Summary guidance</summary>

This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state.

Describe the intent and outcome in 2-5 bullets. Avoid restating the diff; reviewers and bots can read the changed files.

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

</details>

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.

</details>

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
- Real environment tested:
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
- Observed result after fix:
- What was not tested:
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof guidance</summary>

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes. Terminal screenshots, copied live output, redacted runtime logs, recordings, and linked artifacts count.

If your environment cannot produce the ideal proof, explain that under `Proof limitations or environment constraints` so reviewers and ClawSweeper can direct the next step properly.

Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

</details>

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?


What regression coverage was added or updated?


What failed before this fix, if known?


If no test was added, why not?

<details>
<summary>Testing guidance</summary>

List focused commands, not every incidental check. CI is useful support, but external PRs still need real behavior proof above when behavior changes.

</details>

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)


Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)


Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)


What is the highest-risk area?


How is that risk mitigated?

<details>
<summary>Risk guidance</summary>

Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff. ClawSweeper can see touched files, but it cannot know which behavior you think is risky, why the risk is acceptable, or what mitigation reviewers should verify.

</details>

## Current review state

What is the next action?


What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?


Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

<details>
<summary>Review state guidance</summary>

Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

</details>

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve inbound text entities
target.body:
## Summary
- render Telegram inbound formatting entities as markdown before building the agent-facing body
- preserve bold, italic, code, pre, strikethrough, underline, spoiler, and text_link entities using Telegram's original UTF-16 offsets
- add helper and inbound-body regression coverage for formatted Telegram messages

## Verification
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts
- git diff --check

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram inbound formatting entities should be preserved in the agent-facing body by rendering bold, italic, code, pre, strikethrough, underline, spoiler, and text_link entities from Telegram UTF-16 offsets into Markdown before dispatch.
- Real environment tested: Current-head local OpenClaw checkout at `5a603f5822e2e33d806e15cd30a34e2404460a8d` using the actual Telegram `renderTelegramTextEntities` runtime helper called by `resolveTelegramInboundBody`, plus the earlier GitHub Actions Mantis Telegram Desktop Proof workflow on PR head `26ca98e760f0` with private QA enabled.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `gh run view 26072521900 --json databaseId,name,conclusion,status,url,jobs`
  2. GitHub Actions job `Run agentic native Telegram proof` in workflow run `26072521900` executed the native Telegram proof scenario selected by `mantis: telegram-visible-proof`.
  3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
  4. `pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { renderTelegramTextEntities } from "./extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts"; /* invoke bold+link and triple-backtick pre cases */'` from `/home/rev/projects/openclaw-pr-83873-current` at current PR head `5a603f5822e2e33d806e15cd30a34e2404460a8d`.
- Evidence after fix: Live CI/job output and status:
  ```json
  {
    "name": "Mantis Telegram Desktop Proof",
    "run": 26072521900,
    "conclusion": "success",
    "status": "completed",
    "url": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26072521900",
    "jobs": [
      {"name": "Validate selected refs", "conclusion": "success", "url": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26072521900/job/76656751665"},
      {"name": "Run agentic native Telegram proof", "conclusion": "success", "url": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26072521900/job/76656891367"}
    ]
  }
  ```

Current-head local runtime output from the same renderer used by the Telegram inbound body path:

`````text
$ pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { renderTelegramTextEntities } from "./extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts"; /* invoke proof cases */'
HEAD 5a603f5822e2e33d806e15cd30a34e2404460a8d
bold+link input: Read docs now
bold+link output: Read **[docs](https://docs.example)** _now_
pre input:
before
```
inside
pre output:
````md
before
```
inside
````
`````

This directly covers the current-head ClawSweeper ask: bold plus text_link reaches Markdown as `**[docs](https://docs.example)**`, and a Telegram `pre` entity whose content contains triple backticks is rendered with a four-backtick fence so the embedded triple-backtick run cannot close the block.

- Observed result after fix: The earlier native Telegram proof workflow completed successfully for the PR head, including selected-ref validation and the `Run agentic native Telegram proof` job. On current head `5a603f5822e2e33d806e15cd30a34e2404460a8d`, direct invocation of the same Telegram entity renderer used before agent-body construction produced `Read **[docs](https://docs.example)** _now_` for nested bold/text_link/italic entities and used a four-backtick Markdown fence for a `pre` entity whose content contained triple backticks. The targeted vitest command also passed for the Telegram helper/body paths listed in the Verification section.
- What was not tested: I could not run a fresh current-head Mantis Telegram Desktop capture from this account; the workflow dispatch route returned `403` and the comment-triggered workflow reported this actor as `read`, so the native desktop proof remains the earlier repository workflow run. The current-head proof above is direct runtime output from the exact renderer used by the Telegram inbound body path, not a new Telegram Desktop screenshot.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#52859

## Current-head proof refresh (2026-05-19)

Pushed commit `831d938b68c225ae8beda5be12e5a9ec372db8fe` fixes the ClawSweeper `pre` fence blocker by choosing a Markdown fence longer than any backtick run in the Telegram `pre` content, while preserving the existing language-fence behavior.

Commands run at that head:
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts` -> all matched files use the correct format.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts` -> 3 test files passed, 183 tests passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.

Independent diff review passed with no security concerns or logic errors.

## Current-head proof refresh (2026-05-24)

Latest PR head tested: `bbd308644cb3a3eda410df0faf74dd2abaa92193` (empty CI-rerun commit; code under test is unchanged from parent `8d86aefe0b6febc65f136c0d2060cdfa61807de8`).

Commands run at this head:

~~~text
$ node --import tsx <<'NODE'
import { renderTelegramTextEntities } from './extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts';
const text = 'PR83873 docs now\nbefore\n```\ninside';
const entities = [
  { offset: 8, length: 4, type: 'bold' },
  { offset: 8, length: 4, type: 'text_link', url: 'https://docs.example/pr83873' },
  { offset: 17, length: 17, type: 'pre' },
];
console.log(renderTelegramTextEntities(text, entities));
NODE
PR83873 **[docs](https://docs.example/pr83873)** now
````
before
```
inside
````
~~~

Live Telegram Desktop source-message evidence was also captured from the local `Zero` bot DM after sending a formatted message from Telegram Desktop. Redacted source-message cache entry:

~~~json
{
  "source": "Telegram Desktop -> Zero bot DM -> OpenClaw Telegram sourceMessage cache",
  "head": "bbd308644cb3a3eda410df0faf74dd2abaa92193",
  "message": {
    "chat": { "type": "private" },
    "text": "PR83873 docs now\nbefore\n```\ninside",
    "entities": [
      { "offset": 7, "length": 4, "type": "text_link", "url": "https://docs.example/pr83873" },
      { "offset": 17, "length": 17, "type": "pre" }
    ],
    "link_preview_options": { "is_disabled": true }
  }
}
~~~

This proves the live Telegram Desktop path produced Telegram `text_link` and `pre` entities for the bot input, including a `pre` block whose content contains triple backticks. The current-head renderer proof above covers the exact ClawSweeper edge case for overlapping `bold` + `text_link` plus the same triple-backtick `pre` content: it renders `docs` as `**[docs](https://docs.example/pr83873)**` and chooses a four-backtick fence so the embedded triple backticks cannot close the block.

Targeted validation on current head:

~~~text
$ pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       3 passed (3)
Duration    4.47s

$ pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       188 passed (188)
Duration    23.70s
~~~

CI note: the remaining failing GitHub check at the time of this update was `checks-node-agentic-agents`, and the log shows `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timed out after 120s rather than failing an assertion. The same focused test passed locally on this PR head in 4.47s. I attempted to rerun the failed GitHub job, but GitHub returned `run 26370283600 cannot be rerun; Must have admin rights to Repository` for this account.

target.title: fix(daemon): keep node tasks off gateway listener cleanup
target.body:
Summary
- Keep Windows node Scheduled Task stop/restart/status from treating the gateway listener port as node-owned runtime evidence.
- Add installed-command process matching for node Scheduled Task and Startup-folder fallback paths, so node lifecycle commands can still find the managed node host without touching unrelated gateway listeners or manual node runs.
- Cover registered task, Startup fallback, mixed fallback/task, and delayed schtasks launch cases.

Fixes #85289

Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `codex review --uncommitted`

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Windows node Scheduled Task lifecycle no longer reclaims or reports runtime from the Gateway listener port, while still detecting and stopping the installed node host process by matching the installed command line.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 durable OpenClaw worktree at `/root/src/openclaw-85597`; native Windows host `redmond\giodl` with Ubuntu-24.04 WSL2; Windows Task Scheduler (`schtasks`) run/end/rerun/end against task name `OpenClaw Node`; WSL loopback listener reachable from Windows on `127.0.0.1:19897`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the WSL PR checkout at SHA `9204441152acbc9ace41eff1ed369eed053f3935`, ran focused daemon tests and maintainer Testbox below; then started a WSL TCP listener with Windows `Start-Process wsl.exe -u root -- node /tmp/oc85597-listener.js 19897`, created `OpenClaw Node` with `schtasks /Create`, ran `schtasks /Run`, `schtasks /End`, reran, and ended it again while probing `Test-NetConnection 127.0.0.1 -Port 19897` after each lifecycle step.
Evidence after fix: Focused daemon tests passed; Blacksmith Testbox `pnpm check:changed` passed; native Windows/WSL supplemental proof showed `tcp_127.0.0.1_19897=True` after WSL listener start, after node task run, after node task end, after node task rerun, and after node task second end.
Observed result after fix: Node service paths skip gateway listener/port cleanup, ignore unrelated manual node hosts on the same gateway port, and terminate only the matching installed node host command when stop/restart needs process cleanup. In the native Windows/WSL supplemental proof, Windows Task Scheduler lifecycle activity did not disrupt the WSL loopback listener.
What was not tested: full native `openclaw node install/start/stop/restart` against the active Sebby/OpenClaw VM setup. The local native package path was blocked by unrelated environment issues: `pnpm build` currently fails on `protobufjs` descriptor resolution in the shared WSL dependency tree, and the packaged launcher/tsx respawn hits a Windows child-process `Access is denied` failure on this host.

Additional maintainer verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.test.ts src/daemon/service.test.ts src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts` - passed 5 files / 78 tests
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox: `tbx_01ks9f2fmhfar6ahwqvx071pak`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26322648137, command `corepack pnpm check:changed`, exit 0
- Native Windows + WSL2 supplemental proof: local Windows host `redmond\giodl`, Ubuntu-24.04 WSL2, task `OpenClaw Node`, port `19897`; `Test-NetConnection` stayed true after `schtasks /Run`, `schtasks /End`, rerun, and second end; cleanup removed the task and stopped the listener.

target.title: Fix Control UI agent thinking defaults
target.body:
Fixes #81760

## Summary
- include resolved thinking defaults and levels in agents.list rows
- show the resolved thinking default in the Agents overview
- stop chat UI and /think status fallbacks from using global thinking defaults for sessions on a different model

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI should show and inherit the effective thinking default for the selected agent/session, not the global default from a different model.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway from PR head `e4806661795f81e12849864fe227b9f4b5ef92f1` on macOS, with Control UI rebuilt by `pnpm ui:build`, a temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`, token auth on `127.0.0.1:18895`, and a proof config containing:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "model": { "primary": "minimax/MiniMax-M2.7" },
      "thinkingDefault": "off"
    },
    "list": [
      { "id": "main", "default": true },
      {
        "id": "investment-master",
        "model": { "primary": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash" },
        "thinkingDefault": "xhigh"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

For the local headless browser run only, `gateway.controlUi.dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth=true` was set so Chrome could connect over local HTTP without a one-time pairing ceremony. The gateway still used token auth; the behavior under test was the live `agents.list` payload and Control UI rendering.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm ui:build
COREPACK_HOME=/Users/betala/.cache/node/corepack HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81795-home OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81795-home/.openclaw OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81795-home/.openclaw OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81795-home/.openclaw/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-81795 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway --port 18895 --bind loopback --allow-unconfigured --verbose --cli-backend-logs
curl -sS -i http://127.0.0.1:18895/healthz
COREPACK_HOME=/Users/betala/.cache/node/corepack HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81795-home OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81795-home/.openclaw OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81795-home/.openclaw OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81795-home/.openclaw/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-81795 node --import tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-81795-client-proof.ts
node /private/tmp/openclaw-81795-ui-proof.mjs
```

Evidence after fix:

Live gateway output:

```text
$ curl -sS -i http://127.0.0.1:18895/healthz
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8
Cache-Control: no-store
Content-Length: 27

{"ok":true,"status":"live"}

$ node --import tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-81795-client-proof.ts
{
  "gatewayUrl": "ws://127.0.0.1:18895",
  "helloOk": "hello-ok",
  "authRole": "operator",
  "authScopes": ["operator.read"],
  "investmentMaster": {
    "id": "investment-master",
    "model": { "primary": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash" },
    "thinkingDefault": "xhigh",
    "thinkingLevels": ["off", "minimal", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh", "max"],
    "thinkingOptions": ["off", "minimal", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh", "max"]
  }
}
```

Live Control UI browser output:

```text
$ node /private/tmp/openclaw-81795-ui-proof.mjs
{
  "url": "http://127.0.0.1:18895/agents",
  "matchedExpectedText": true,
  "sawInvestmentMaster": true,
  "sawThinkingDefault": true,
  "sawXhigh": true,
  "thinkingSnippet": "Workspace ... /private/tmp/openclaw-proof-81795-workspace/investment-master\nPrimary Model\ndeepseek/deepseek-v4-flash\nRuntime\nauto\nThinking Default\nxhigh\nSkills Filter\nall skills ...",
  "consoleMessages": [
    "debug: [openclaw] control-ui.rpc {... method: agents.list, ok: true, durationMs: 235, slow: false}",
    "debug: [openclaw] control-ui.rpc {... method: agents.list, ok: true, durationMs: 74, slow: false}"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: The running gateway returns the `investment-master` row with `thinkingDefault: "xhigh"` and model-specific thinking levels instead of the global `off` default. The running Control UI `/agents` page, connected to that gateway, renders the selected `investment-master` overview with `Thinking Default` shown as `xhigh`.

What was not tested: I did not run a live LLM chat turn or `/think` command against a provider because the proof config intentionally has no provider API keys. The focused tests below cover the chat selector and `/think` fallback paths.

Re-verified on current head `634b90d5a2e422e778592949e07d9339fba1095b` (2026-05-25): the branch was rebased onto current `origin/main`, reconciling `resolveThinkingLevelOptions` with main's added model-catalog argument while keeping the `defaultFromSessionDefaults` fix. The live gateway and Control UI transcripts above were captured on the pre-rebase head `e4806661795f81e12849864fe227b9f4b5ef92f1`; the rebase only reconciled the merge and did not change the user-facing behavior under test. The focused regression suite re-runs green on the current head:

```console
$ pnpm test src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/slash-command-executor.node.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts   -> Test Files 1 passed (1)  Tests 94 passed (94)
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts        -> Test Files 1 passed (1)  Tests 8 passed (8)
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-ui.config.ts   -> Test Files 2 passed (2)  Tests 86 passed (86)
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards
```

## Verification
- pnpm test src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/slash-command-executor.node.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/shared/session-types.ts src/gateway/session-utils.types.ts src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents-panels-overview.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/slash-command-executor.ts ui/src/ui/chat/slash-command-executor.node.test.ts
- git diff --check
- pnpm ui:build
- live gateway RPC and live Control UI browser transcripts above
